html:
[209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鐵人 - Refers to a statue or figurine made of iron, often with religious or magical connotations.
  • 高尺許 - Means 'about a foot tall,' using an approximate measure.
  • 左手擎钵 - Describes the iron figure holding a bowl in its left hand.
  • 注水，移时自沸 - Means 'pouring water into it, after a while it boils on its own,' indicating a magical or supernatural property.
  • 數易皆然 - Means 'even if the water is changed multiple times, it still boils,' emphasizing the consistent effect.
  • 不以示人 - Means 'not showing it to others,' indicating the owner's secrecy.
  • 搬弄不已 - Means 'handling it without end,' describing the owner's frequent interaction with the figure.
  • 忽誤觸手钵 - Means 'accidentally knocked the bowl with his hand,' describing an unintended action.
  • 脫底 - Means 'the bottom fell off,' indicating the bowl broke.
  • 復盛水 - Means 'refilled with water,' describing the action of pouring water into the bowl again.
  • 其下鐫“諸葛亮造”四隸字 - Means 'on the bottom were carved four clerical script characters 'Zhuge Liang made,' indicating the inscription.
  • 鐵人掌心鑄一“火”字 - Means 'on the palm of the iron figure was cast a 'fire' character,' describing the additional inscription.
  • 再注之水，則冷然也 - Means 'when water was poured into it again, it became cold,' indicating a change in the figure's properties.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高密陰城 - Refers to Yincheng, a place in Gaomi, Shandong Province, China.
  • 諸葛亮 - Refers to Zhuge Liang, a famous military strategist and statesman during the Three Kingdoms period.

Proper Nouns

  • 高密陰城 - Yincheng, a place in Gaomi, Shandong Province, China.
  • 諸葛亮 - Zhuge Liang, a famous military strategist and statesman during the Three Kingdoms period.

[210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及长，名葆 - When he grew up, his name was Bao.
  • 茹素 - To eat a vegetarian diet.
  • 强以荤酒，辄呕吐 - When forced to eat meat and drink alcohol, he would vomit.
  • 聪慧 - Intelligent and wise.
  • 事母孝 - Filial to his mother.
  • 入泮 - Refers to entering the school, a rite of passage in ancient China.
  • 领乡荐 - To be recommended for the provincial examination.
  • 联捷南宫 - To successively pass the imperial examinations and enter the Hanlin Academy.
  • 博闻强识 - Having a broad knowledge and strong memory.
  • 精通释典 - Proficient in Buddhist scriptures.
  • 授中翰 - Appointed as a Hanlin scholar.
  • 罣叱 - To scold or rebuke.
  • 天地间亦知有我否 - Do you know that there is me in the world?
  • 一真二谛 - Refers to the two truths in Buddhist philosophy: conventional truth and ultimate truth.
  • 三摩四大 - Refers to the four elements (earth, water, fire, air) in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 五蕴六欲 - Refers to the five aggregates (form, feeling, perception, mental formations, consciousness) and the six desires (sight, sound, smell, taste, touch, thought).
  • 七心八垢 - Refers to the seven minds and eight impurities in Buddhist psychology.
  • 九根十行 - Refers to the nine faculties and ten practices in Buddhist ethics.
  • 恬静无为 - Tranquil and non-action, a Daoist principle.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 慈仁寺 - Refers to the Ciren Temple in Beijing, a famous Buddhist temple.
  • 浮屠 - Refers to a Buddhist monk or a pagoda.
  • 大善知识 - Refers to a highly respected Buddhist teacher.
  • 无上《妙法》诸经 - Refers to the highest and most profound Buddhist scriptures.
  • 中翰 - Refers to a position in the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution for scholars in the imperial court.
  • 荆州 - Refers to Jingzhou, a historical region in central China.
  • 中庸 - Refers to the Doctrine of the Mean, a central concept in Confucian philosophy.
  • 释典 - Refers to Buddhist scriptures.
  • 浮屠 - Refers to a Buddhist monk or a pagoda.
  • 大善知识 - Refers to a highly respected Buddhist teacher.
  • 无上《妙法》诸经 - Refers to the highest and most profound Buddhist scriptures.
  • 罣叱 - Refers to scolding or rebuking.
  • 天地间亦知有我否 - Refers to a question about one's existence in the world.
  • 一真二谛 - Refers to the two truths in Buddhist philosophy.
  • 三摩四大 - Refers to the four elements in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 五蕴六欲 - Refers to the five aggregates and six desires in Buddhist psychology.
  • 七心八垢 - Refers to the seven minds and eight impurities in Buddhist psychology.
  • 九根十行 - Refers to the nine faculties and ten practices in Buddhist ethics.
  • 恬静无为 - Refers to a Daoist principle of tranquility and non-action.
  • 郡人颂之 - Refers to the praise of the people in a prefecture.
  • 圣贤功用 - Refers to the functions of sages and virtuous people.
  • 主敬主静 - Refers to maintaining reverence and tranquility.
  • 道释两家 - Refers to the Daoist and Buddhist schools.
  • 中庸极致 - Refers to the highest point of the Doctrine of the Mean.
  • 偏倚 - Refers to bias or skew.
  • 浅尝者 - Refers to those who have only superficially tasted.
  • 訾病 - Refers to criticizing or finding fault.
  • 释子 - Refers to Buddhist monks.
  • 古之佛氏之罪人 - Refers to the sinners of ancient Buddhism.
  • 道士 - Refers to Daoist priests.
  • 古之老子之罪人 - Refers to the sinners of ancient Daoism.
  • 秀才 - Refers to scholars or literati.
  • 古之圣人之罪人 - Refers to the sinners of ancient sages.

Proper Nouns

  • 慈仁寺 - The Ciren Temple in Beijing.
  • 浮屠 - A Buddhist monk or a pagoda.
  • 大善知识 - A highly respected Buddhist teacher.
  • 无上《妙法》诸经 - The highest and most profound Buddhist scriptures.
  • 中翰 - A position in the Hanlin Academy.
  • 荆州 - A historical region in central China.
  • 释典 - Buddhist scriptures.
  • 浮屠 - A Buddhist monk or a pagoda.
  • 大善知识 - A highly respected Buddhist teacher.
  • 无上《妙法》诸经 - The highest and most profound Buddhist scriptures.
  • 罣叱 - Scolding or rebuking.
  • 天地间亦知有我否 - A question about one's existence in the world.
  • 一真二谛 - The two truths in Buddhist philosophy.
  • 三摩四大 - The four elements in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 五蕴六欲 - The five aggregates and six desires in Buddhist psychology.
  • 七心八垢 - The seven minds and eight impurities in Buddhist psychology.
  • 九根十行 - The nine faculties and ten practices in Buddhist ethics.
  • 恬静无为 - Tranquil and non-action, a Daoist principle.

[211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 郡城外有一古寺 - Sets the scene in a typical Chinese setting, where temples are often located outside city walls.
  • 内有泥鬼 - Concept of a clay demon rooted in Chinese folklore and mythology, where inanimate objects can come to life.
  • 乡人咸惊异 - Villagers' reaction reflects the common belief in supernatural beings and the fear they inspire.
  • 祀以香花 - Worship with incense and flowers is a traditional practice in Chinese culture, symbolizing respect and devotion.
  • 日盛其事 - Increasing complexity of the worship ritual shows the growing importance of the clay demon in the villagers' lives.
  • 金舆往视 - Gold Cart is a character representing a figure of authority or wisdom, often found in classical Chinese literature.
  • 只这是泥是土 - Gold Cart's dismissal of the clay demon's powers reflects a rational and skeptical worldview.
  • 何圣何灵 - Rhetorical question emphasizes the absurdity of the clay demon's supposed supernatural abilities.
  • 速毁而瘗之 - Command to destroy and bury the clay demon shows Gold Cart's decisive and practical approach.
  • 夜一青衣来拜 - Appearance of a green-clad figure at night is a common motif in Chinese ghost stories, symbolizing the supernatural.
  • 我乃山鬼 - Self-identification as a mountain ghost is a reference to Chinese mythology, where mountain spirits are often associated with natural disasters and curses.
  • 久受孽报 - Concept of suffering under the curse of sin is a common theme in Chinese religious and philosophical thought.
  • 蒙君打脱一切障碍 - Expression of gratitude to Gold Cart for freeing the mountain ghost from its curse shows the interconnectedness of human and supernatural realms.
  • 如听无生大乘 - Religious phrase suggests a profound spiritual experience, reflecting the influence of Buddhism on Chinese culture.
  • 谢而去 - Departure of the mountain ghost after expressing gratitude adds a sense of closure.
  • 三年，母死归葬 - Significant personal event in Gold Cart's life, marking the death of his mother and his return to bury her.
  • 庐于墓旁 - Gold Cart's decision to live near his mother's grave, building a hut by the grave.
  • 服阕不仕 - Gold Cart's decision to remain in seclusion after his mother's death, not taking up office after the mourning period.
  • 有僧自杭来 - Introduction of a new character, a monk from Hangzhou.
  • 门隶呵之 - Initial reaction of the gatekeeper to the monk, rebuking him.
  • 僧遗扇一柄 - Monk leaves behind a fan as a gift to Gold Cart.
  • 门者呈金 - Gatekeeper presents the fan to Gold Cart.
  • 长老命我归矣 - Monk's message to Gold Cart, ordered by the elder to return.
  • 夫人李氏 - Introduction of Gold Cart's wife, Lady Li.
  • 旧家阀阅之女 - Description of Lady Li's background, a daughter of a prominent family.
  • 闻金欲之杭为僧 - Lady Li's reaction to Gold Cart's plans to become a monk in Hangzhou.
  • 从容而进词 - Lady Li's composed and persuasive manner in presenting her words.
  • 妾闻达者明理而通变 - Lady Li's opening statement, emphasizing the importance of wisdom and flexibility.
  • 愚人守暗而抱拙 - Contrast between the enlightened and the foolish, emphasizing the importance of wisdom.
  • 人生世上，不过忠孝节义诸大端 - Summary of the core values of life, emphasizing loyalty, filial piety, integrity, and righteousness.
  • 今欲去先人之墓庐 - Addressing Gold Cart's desire to leave his mother's grave, questioning the morality of his actions.
  • 可谓孝乎 - Questioning the morality of Gold Cart's actions, asking if leaving the tomb can be called filial.
  • 当此承平 - Setting the context of the current era, emphasizing peaceful times.
  • 不思鼓吹休明以和其盛 - Criticism of Gold Cart's lack of engagement in public affairs, suggesting he should promote peace and prosperity.
  • 而乃遁逃枯槁 - Description of Gold Cart's choice to live in seclusion, fleeing to a life of poverty and hardship.
  • 可谓忠乎 - Questioning the morality of Gold Cart's actions, asking if leaving home can be called loyal.
  • 况乎里闾推重 - Highlighting the community's high regard for Gold Cart, emphasizing the importance of family and tradition.
  • 后生矜式 - Emphasizing the influence Gold Cart has on the younger generation, looking up to him as a role model.
  • 正赖父兄之董 - Suggesting the importance of family and tradition, relying on the guidance of father and elder brothers.
  • 率为乡先生之规 - Emphasizing the importance of following established norms, following the rules of the village elders.
  • 以绵世泽 - Goal of maintaining the family's legacy, continuing the family's reputation.
  • 以熏善良 - Emphasizing the positive impact of Gold Cart's actions, inspiring goodness.
  • 愿夫子不出家而成教于国也 - Lady Li's final plea to Gold Cart, urging him to stay and contribute to society.
  • 金怃然为间 - Gold Cart's emotional response to Lady Li's words, deeply moved and pausing.
  • 深以为是 - Gold Cart's acceptance of Lady Li's advice, deeply agreeing with her.
  • 乃告夫人作杭州之游 - Gold Cart's decision to go to Hangzhou, telling Lady Li about his trip.
  • 以了前因 - Purpose of the trip, resolving the previous cause.
  • 仍归故里 - Gold Cart's ultimate decision to return home, returning to his hometown.
  • 遂不为世外想 - Gold Cart's final state of mind, no longer thinking of the outside world, committed to his family and community.

[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 抵杭询长老 - Literally 'arrived in Hangzhou to consult with elder monks,' indicating a visit to seek information or guidance.
  • 卓锡去矣 - A Buddhist idiom meaning 'to leave (a place) with a staff,' implying that the monk has departed.
  • 启龛视之 - Literally 'opened the niche to inspect it,' referring to the act of examining something stored in a niche or shrine.
  • 面目如生 - A common expression meaning 'the face looks as if alive,' describing someone who has been preserved in a lifelike manner.
  • 火化之期，妙香四闻，祥光烛天 - Describes the miraculous events surrounding the cremation, including the fragrance spreading far and bright light illuminating the sky.
  • 修身立行 - Literally 'cultivating oneself and establishing one's conduct,' referring to moral self-improvement and ethical behavior.
  • 二子皆成进士 - Literally 'both sons became jinshi (imperial scholars),' indicating that both sons passed the highest level of the imperial examinations.
  • 遂入程朱之室 - Literally 'then entered the room of Cheng and Zhu,' meaning that the person adopted the teachings of Cheng Yi and Zhu Xi, two prominent Neo-Confucian scholars.
  • 著《潜修录》十卷 - Literally 'wrote *Qianxiu Lu* in ten volumes,' referring to the authorship of a book titled *Qianxiu Lu*.
  • 语皆精粹可宝也 - Literally 'all the words are exquisite and precious,' praising the quality of the writing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长老 - Refers to senior monks or spiritual leaders.
  • 寺僧 - Refers to monks at a temple.
  • 三月前 - Refers to a period of three months, indicating a recent event.
  • 火化 - Refers to cremation, a common practice in Buddhism.
  • 妙香四闻 - Refers to a fragrant aroma spreading widely, often associated with spiritual or miraculous events.
  • 祥光烛天 - Refers to a bright, auspicious light illuminating the sky, often seen as a sign of divine presence or blessing.
  • 封之后山 - Refers to sealing the remains in a mountain, a common burial practice in Chinese culture.
  • 再来人 - Refers to a person who has returned from the afterlife or a higher state of existence, often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 程朱之室 - Refers to the teachings of Cheng Yi and Zhu Xi, two influential Neo-Confucian philosophers.
  • 进士 - Refers to individuals who have passed the highest level of the imperial examinations, indicating high scholarly achievement.
  • 潜修录 - Refers to a book titled *Qianxiu Lu*, which means 'Record of Hidden Cultivation' or 'Record of Secret Practice.'

Proper Nouns

  • 金 - The name of the person being described.
  • 长老 - Refers to senior monks or spiritual leaders.
  • 寺僧 - Refers to monks at a temple.
  • 程朱 - Refers to Cheng Yi and Zhu Xi, two prominent Neo-Confucian scholars.
  • 潜修录 - Refers to the book *Qianxiu Lu*.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幼颖悟 - From a young age, he was quick-witted and insightful.
  • 不茹荤 - Literally means 'does not eat meat,' often used to indicate vegetarianism or a pure diet.
  • 丧父母 - Lost his parents.
  • 依叔 - Relied on his uncle.
  • 叔母不能容 - The uncle's wife could not tolerate him.
  • 认寡妇游氏为母 - Acknowledged a widow named You as his mother.
  • 拳以杖画地 - With his fist, he drew in the ground with a stick.
  • 牛不他逸 - The cattle did not wander away.
  • 烹鱼啖之 - Cooked fish and fed it to them.
  • 受而投诸水 - Took it and threw it back into the water.
  • 黑质白章，尾上焦 - Black body with white patterns, the tail burned.
  • 遗种也 - The remaining offspring.
  • 爱山水之胜 - Loved the beauty of the mountains and waters.
  • 欲结茅于此 - Wanted to build a thatched hut here.
  • 不果 - Did not succeed.
  • 游母既没 - After the mother You passed away.
  • 拳营窀穸 - With his own hands, he prepared the grave.
  • 后遂去 - Later, he left.
  • 里人为之筑灵觉寺 - The villagers built the Lingjue Temple for him.
  • 继之砌上 - Later, they added a platform.
  • 莆田有二寺 - In Putian, there are two temples.
  • 相传皆拳卓锡取泉处 - It is said that both were places where he obtained water by striking the ground.
  • 至神泉市 - Arrived at Shenquan Market.
  • 欲济无舟 - Wanted to cross but had no boat.
  • 折苇以渡 - Broke reeds to make a raft to cross.
  • 登黄龙献爪山 - Climbed Mount Huanglong Xianzhua.
  • 循顶西行 - Followed the top westward.
  • 抵平沙社之楠树坑 - Reached the Nan Shu Pit in Pingsha Village.
  • 依袁姓三年 - Lived with the surname Yuan for three years.
  • 后人因其地为高砌寺 - Later, people built the Gaoqi Temple at that place.
  • 爰乃涉芒州岗之巅 - Then, he climbed to the top of Mangzhou Hill.
  • 西望阴那五峰蝉联 - Looked westward at the five peaks of Yinna, linked like cicadas.
  • 耸峙云表 - Towering high in the clouds.
  • 神赏者久之 - Admired it for a long time.
  • 欣然欲往 - Gladly wanted to go there.
  • 过浒梓村 - Passed through Huzi Village.
  • 求水弗得 - Tried to find water but failed.
  • 乃卓锡成井 - Then, he struck the ground with his staff to create a well.
  • 有石龟 - There was a stone turtle.
  • 至今存焉 - It still exists today.
  • 虽亢旱 - Even during severe drought.
  • 泉不竭 - The spring does not dry up.
  • 后亦建灵山亭 - Later, they built the Lingshan Pavilion.
  • 拳像塑其中 - A statue of him was placed inside.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大埔县 - Refers to Dabu County in Guangdong Province.
  • 黑质白章，尾上焦 - Refers to a specific type of fish, possibly a metaphor for a unique or special creature.
  • 灵觉寺 - Refers to the Lingjue Temple, a place of worship and remembrance.
  • 高砌寺 - Refers to the Gaoqi Temple, a place of religious significance.
  • 神泉市 - Refers to Shenquan Market, a place known for its natural springs.
  • 黄龙献爪山 - Refers to Mount Huanglong Xianzhua, a mountain with cultural and possibly religious significance.
  • 平沙社之楠树坑 - Refers to the Nan Shu Pit in Pingsha Village, a specific location.
  • 阴那五峰 - Refers to the five peaks of Yinna, a scenic and possibly sacred area.
  • 灵山亭 - Refers to the Lingshan Pavilion, a place of worship and remembrance.
  • 拳像 - Refers to a statue of the person described in the text, likely a revered figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 大埔县 - Dabu County, a place in Guangdong Province.
  • 黄砂社车上村 - Huangsha She Checun, a specific village.
  • 游氏 - A widow named You, who became the subject's mother.
  • 赤嶡岭 - Chixi Ridge, a mountain.
  • 灵觉寺 - Lingjue Temple, a place of worship.
  • 高砌寺 - Gaoqi Temple, a place of religious significance.
  • 神泉市 - Shenquan Market, a place known for its natural springs.
  • 黄龙献爪山 - Mount Huanglong Xianzhua, a mountain.
  • 平沙社之楠树坑 - Nan Shu Pit in Pingsha Village, a specific location.
  • 阴那五峰 - The five peaks of Yinna, a scenic and possibly sacred area.
  • 灵山亭 - Lingshan Pavilion, a place of worship.
  • 拳像 - A statue of the subject.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至陰那 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location, possibly a mountain or temple site.
  • 斲石刊木 - Literally 'chopping stone and carving wood,' referring to the construction of a temple or hermitage.
  • 建道場為修真地 - Means 'building a temple as a place for cultivating the true self,' where '道場' refers to a religious or spiritual center.
  • 日说法，众多不省 - Means 'daily preaching, but many did not understand,' indicating that despite the master's teachings, the audience struggled to grasp the deeper meanings.
  • 曾赋诗曰 - Means 'once composed a poem saying,' introducing a poetic expression.
  • 行脚腰包廿载游 - Literally 'wandering for twenty years with a simple pack,' describing a life of travel and simplicity.
  • 一天花坠雨成秋 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the fleeting nature of life or the passage of time.
  • 指禅未觉羞拳了 - A Buddhist phrase, likely referring to the practice of Zen (禅) and the idea that even pointing to the truth (指禅) may not fully convey it.
  • 顽石因何不点头 - A metaphorical question, possibly referring to the idea that even inanimate objects (like stones) can respond to spiritual teachings.
  • 住阴那又三十年 - Means 'stayed at Yinna for another thirty years,' indicating a long period of residence.
  • 从前佛祖皆宏演法乘，自度以度人 - Means 'In the past, all Buddhas widely preached the Dharma, saving themselves to save others,' emphasizing the dual role of self-cultivation and helping others.
  • 我未能也，心甚愧之 - Means 'I could not do this, and I feel deeply ashamed,' expressing the master's humility and self-criticism.
  • 圆寂后，藏我骸于塔 - Means 'After my passing, bury my remains in a stupa,' referring to the traditional Buddhist practice of cremation and enshrinement.
  • 当颜其额曰‘惭愧’ - Means 'Mark the top of the stupa with the words 'candkui' (shame and gratitude),' indicating the master's desire to acknowledge his imperfections.
  • 因偈曰 - Means 'Thus, I composed a verse saying,' introducing a final poetic expression.
  • 四十九年，无系无牵 - Means 'Forty-nine years, without attachment or entanglement,' referring to the master's long life of detachment.
  • 如今撒手归空去，万里云开月在天 - A metaphorical expression, likely referring to the master's death and the idea of returning to the void or the ultimate truth.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阴那 - A specific place, possibly a mountain or temple site, where the master lived and taught.
  • 道场 - A religious or spiritual center, often used in Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 修真地 - A place for cultivating the true self, emphasizing spiritual practice.
  • 佛祖 - Refers to the Buddha and other enlightened beings in Buddhism.
  • 法乘 - Refers to the Dharma, the teachings of the Buddha.
  • 圆寂 - A Buddhist term for the death of an enlightened being, often translated as 'passing away' or 'entering Nirvana.'
  • 塔 - A stupa, a type of Buddhist monument used for enshrining relics or the remains of a master.
  • 偈 - A short verse or poem, often used in Buddhist teachings.

Proper Nouns

  • 阴那 - A specific place, possibly a mountain or temple site.
  • 佛祖 - Refers to the Buddha and other enlightened beings in Buddhism.
  • 塔 - A stupa, a type of Buddhist monument.
  • 偈 - A short verse or poem used in Buddhist teachings.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 没后屡显灵异 - Posthumous manifestation of supernatural abilities, common in Chinese folklore and religious texts.
  • 明御史梅鼎臣 - Ming censor Mei Dingchen, a historical figure.
  • 舟过蓬辣滩 - Boat passing over Pengla Tuan, a specific location on a river.
  • 水汹涌，舟几覆 - Water surging, boat nearly capsized, describing a dangerous situation.
  • 见老僧于岸，隐约指点 - Seeing an old monk on the shore, vaguely pointing, indicating a mysterious guidance.
  • 舟得无恙 - The boat remained unharmed, meaning the boat was safe.
  • 三饶寇乱 - Three Ráo bandit troubles, referring to a period of unrest in the Three Ráo region.
  • 时过阴那 - Passing through Yīn Nà, indicating a specific location.
  • 将肆虏掠 - About to commit plunder, suggesting an impending attack.
  • 忽云雾四塞，咫尺不辨人 - Suddenly clouds and mist filled all around, making it impossible to see even at arm's length, describing a sudden and dense fog.
  • 贼迷失道路，各村赖以全 - The bandits lost their way, and each village was saved, indicating the villages were protected by the fog.
  • 每至三月，山中必风雨 - Every March, there must be rain and wind in the mountains, describing a seasonal pattern.
  • 相传洗殿 - It is said that the temple is washed, possibly referring to a ritual or natural phenomenon.
  • 盖了拳生于元和十二年三月也 - It is said that Leiqüan was born in the third month of the twelfth year of Yuanhe, indicating a specific birth date.
  • 余十五岁游阴那 - I traveled to Yīn Nà at the age of fifteen, indicating a personal experience.
  • 见冥然僧时挂搭于此 - Seeing a monk named Míng Rán who was staying here, indicating a meeting with a monk.
  • 相与谈宴弥日 - We talked and feasted for the whole day, describing a long and enjoyable conversation.
  • 闽人 - A person from Fujian, indicating the monk's origin.
  • 山多大笋 - The mountains have many large bamboo shoots, describing the local flora.
  • 径围尺许 - With a circumference of about a foot, indicating the size of the bamboo shoots.

This phrase explains the nun's background.
  • 谓老尼曰：“此师叔，为我姑姊妹行。” - 谓老尼曰 means told the old nun, 此师叔 means this is my aunt, and 为我姑姊妹行 means she is my aunt, sister, or cousin. This phrase clarifies the relationship.
  • 饭生，生以香资与老尼，老尼喜曰：“郎君我师侄友也。今薄暮，盍与吾侄作抵足谈？” - 饭生 means fed him, 生以香资与老尼 means he gave her incense money, 老尼喜曰 means the old nun happily said, 郎君我师侄友也 means you are my nephew and friend, 今薄暮 means now it is late afternoon, and 盍与吾侄作抵足谈 means why don't we have a long talk? This phrase describes the hospitality and invitation for a conversation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阿进年十六，杭人，父幕死于扬 - Biographical opening stating that 'Ah Jin, aged sixteen, is from Hangzhou, and his father died in Yangzhou.' The phrase '父幕' can be interpreted as 'father's career' or 'father's work,' but it is ambiguous without further context.
  • 亦娟好，兼通书算、弹丝 - Describes Ah Jin as 'graceful and also proficient in arithmetic, calculation, and playing stringed instruments.'
  • 喜不自胜，携归，如载宝而来 - Expresses Ah Jin's joy upon being brought back, likening it to carrying treasure.
  • 盛氏闻之，先舆来迎而归 - States that the Sheng family heard of this and sent a carriage to welcome Ah Jin back.
  • 风涛雨雪，带水环山，适可而止 - Describes the natural environment as 'wind, waves, rain, and snow, with water and mountains surrounding, stopping at the right moment.' The phrase '适可而止' can be interpreted as 'stop at the right moment' or 'know when to stop.'
  • 今家迄小康，不劳更作行旅想矣 - States that 'now the family has reached a state of moderate prosperity, and there is no need to think about traveling again.'
  • 董遂鬻田百亩，构一精舍，环以竹木，经岁不出，悠然怡乐 - Describes Dong as selling a hundred acres of land to build a refined pavilion surrounded by bamboo and trees, remaining there for years, living in leisure and joy.
  • 一妻三妾，爱若同胞 - States that Dong has one wife and three concubines, loving them as if they were siblings. The phrase '爱若同胞' can be interpreted as 'love as if they were siblings' or 'treat as equals.'
  • 衣则易着，食则共器，既偶俱之无猜，更相期以共济 - Describes the family's lifestyle as 'wearing clothes easily, eating from the same dishes, having no jealousy among the spouses, and expecting mutual support.' The phrase '既偶俱之无猜' can be interpreted as 'having no jealousy among the spouses' or 'being free from suspicion.'
  • 一家之中，盛氏总其成，暖云司纫事，兰绡佐中馈，阿进操会计书写，分任焉 - Describes the roles within the family: 'Sheng oversees the household, Nuanyun manages sewing, Lanxiao assists in cooking, and Ah Jin handles accounting and writing.'
  • 皆擅其长，即错综焉，亦无废事 - States that 'all excel in their respective duties, even when tasks are complex, nothing is neglected.'
  • 凡有妇人入董门者，皆薰其德而化为善 - Describes how 'any woman entering Dong's household is influenced by his virtue and becomes good.'
  • 一时子玉之邻无诟谇声 - States that 'at that time, there were no complaints or disputes in the neighborhood of Ziyu.'
  • 每见夫妻燕好，一有小星，顿起参商 - Describes how 'when couples are intimate, even a small star can cause discord.' The phrase '参商' refers to the constellations of Shang and Shen, often used metaphorically to describe estrangement or discord.
  • 甚至林间狮吼，岭上鹃啼 - Describes extreme situations where 'even a roar of a lion in the forest or a cry of a cuckoo on the hill can cause unrest.'
  • 借使三善能称，而一夫作难，又安得保此庸庸之福也哉 - States that 'even if three virtues are praised, if one person causes trouble, how can one maintain such ordinary happiness?'
  • 说者谓董子善齐家，余谓董子有修身之道 - Describes the opinion that 'some say Dong is good at managing his household, but I say Dong has the way of self-cultivation.'
  • 想其被面障扇时，其气象固已异矣 - Describes how 'when he was covered by a fan, his demeanor was already different.'
  • 使董子出而为仕，化家为国，正有可观 - States that 'if Dong were to enter public service, transforming his family into a model for the nation, it would indeed be impressive.' The phrase '化家为国' can be interpreted as 'transforming his family into a model for the nation' or 'turning his household into a national example.'
  • 噫！董子不以轩冕之荣，易此闺房之乐也 - Concludes with a sigh that 'Dong does not trade the honor of high office for this domestic happiness.' The phrase '轩冕之荣' refers to the honor and prestige associated with high office or noble titles.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 盛氏，膠東人，號淑娟 - 盛氏是姓氏，膠東人指其來自膠東地區，號淑娟是其別號，表示她有別號或字。
  • 修眉方面，性恬靜，好佛 - 修眉方面形容女子眉目清秀，性恬靜表示性格平和，好佛表示她信仰佛教。
  • 瀹茗 - 瀹茗指泡茶，是中國傳統文化中的一種休閒活動。
  • 焚香缕缕 - 焚香指燒香，缕缕形容香煙縷縷升起，營造出一種寧靜的氛圍。
  • 暖云，苏人，幼鬻于董 - 暖云是人名，苏人表示她來自蘇州，幼鬻于董表示她幼時被賣為奴隸。
  • 身瘦怯如春月柳，微有白麻 - 身瘦怯如春月柳形容她體態纖細，微有白麻表示她面色微白。
  • 足缠似锥，爱着通绣红履 - 足缠似锥形容她腳部纏足，爱着通绣红履表示她喜歡穿紅色刺繡鞋。
  • 善针工，一家衣着袜鞋出其手 - 善针工表示她擅長刺繡，一家衣着袜鞋出其手表示她為全家縫製衣物和鞋襪。
  • 盛氏所供髮绣观音像，暖云之制也 - 盛氏所供髮绣观音像指盛氏家族供奉的髮繡觀音像，暖云之制也表示這件作品是由暖云製作的。
  • 兰绡，扬州人 - 兰绡是人名，扬州人表示她來自揚州。
  • 目秀准直，心灵警，口滑稽，能令人善听 - 目秀准直形容她眼睛秀美，心灵警表示她心思敏捷，口滑稽表示她言辭幽默，能令人善听表示她能吸引他人注意。
  • 精于庖厨，每饭一蔬，多出奇想 - 精于庖厨表示她廚藝精湛，每饭一蔬，多出奇想表示她每頓飯都能做出創意十足的菜餚。
  • 如以紫玉光熯肉、荷叶粉鱼炸、醋浸山兰蕊、木瓜花作酱，皆味外味云 - 如以紫玉光熯肉、荷叶粉鱼炸、醋浸山兰蕊、木瓜花作酱，皆味外味云描述了幾道特色菜餚及其獨特的風味。
  • 阿进，钱唐人 - 阿进是人名，钱唐人表示她來自錢塘（今杭州）。
  • 体丰而软，好洁，喜穿青蓝，以显其白 - 体丰而软形容她體態豐滿，好洁表示她講究衛生，喜穿青蓝表示她喜歡穿藍色和綠色的衣服，以显其白表示她以此襯托自己的白皙皮膚。
  • 古人谓丰若有余、柔若无骨是也 - 古人谓丰若有余、柔若无骨是也形容她體態豐滿且柔軟，如同無骨一般。
  • 能书算，一家度支悉付之 - 能书算表示她擅長書寫和計算，一家度支悉付之表示她負責全家的財務管理。
  • 暇时洗桐拭竹，扫径浇花，绝无一毫俗韵 - 暇时洗桐拭竹，扫径浇花，绝无一毫俗韵描述了她空閒時的活動，如清潔樂器、掃地澆花，表現出一種高雅的品味。
  • 或于灯前月下，一家环坐，听阿进弄阮弦唱平湖调，文词数折，其音袅袅，如流莺云 - 或于灯前月下，一家环坐，听阿进弄阮弦唱平湖调，文词数折，其音袅袅，如流莺云描述了她夜晚與家人圍坐聽她彈奏阮琴並演唱平湖調的場景，其聲音柔美如黃鶯。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盛氏，胶东人，号淑娟 - 盛氏來自膠東地區，號淑娟是其別號，反映了古代中國使用別號或字的習俗。
  • 修眉方面，性恬静，好佛 - 描述了一位性格平和且信仰佛教的女子，反映了佛教在古代中國社會中的重要地位。
  • 瀹茗 - 泡茶是中國傳統文化中的一種休閒活動，常與閒適和思考聯繫在一起。
  • 焚香缕缕 - 燒香是中國宗教和文化儀式中的常見實踐，象徵著純潔和精神聯繫。
  • 暖云，苏人，幼鬻于董 - 暖云幼時被賣為奴隸，反映了當時社會和經濟條件。
  • 身瘦怯如春月柳，微有白麻 - 描述了一位體態纖細、面色微白的女子，反映了古代中國的審美標準。
  • 足缠似锥，爱着通绣红履 - 足部纏足是古代中國的常見習俗，穿紅色刺繡鞋反映了當時的時尚和地位。
  • 善针工，一家衣着袜鞋出其手 - 擅長刺繡在古代中國家庭中非常重要，縫製全家的衣物和鞋襪反映了家庭技能的重要性。
  • 盛氏所供髮绣观音像，暖云之制也 - 髮繡觀音像的供奉和製作反映了當時的宗教和藝術實踐。
  • 兰绡，扬州人 - 揚州在古代中國具有文化和經濟上的重要性。
  • 目秀准直，心灵警，口滑稽，能令人善听 - 描述了一位眼睛秀美、心思敏捷、言辭幽默且能吸引他人注意的女子，反映了當時對多才多藝個體的理想。
  • 精于庖厨，每饭一蔬，多出奇想 - 廚藝精湛和創意十足的菜餚反映了古代中國對飲食和待客的重視。
  • 如以紫玉光熯肉、荷叶粉鱼炸、醋浸山兰蕊、木瓜花作酱，皆味外味云 - 描述了幾道特色菜餚及其獨特的風味，反映了古代中國菜餚的多樣性和精緻。
  • 阿进，钱唐人 - 錢塘（今杭州）在古代中國具有文化和經濟上的重要性。
  • 体丰而软，好洁，喜穿青蓝，以显其白 - 描述了一位體態豐滿、講究衛生、喜歡穿藍色和綠色衣服的女子，反映了當時的審美和衛生標準。
  • 古人谓丰若有余、柔若无骨是也 - 形容她體態豐滿且柔軟，如同無骨一般，反映了當時對某種體態美的欣賞。
  • 能书算，一家度支悉付之 - 擅長書寫和計算並負責全家財務管理，反映了教育和財務管理在古代中國家庭中的重要性。
  • 暇时洗桐拭竹，扫径浇花，绝无一毫俗韵 - 描述了她空閒時的活動，如清潔樂器、掃地澆花，表現出一種高雅的品味。
  • 或于灯前月下，一家环坐，听阿进弄阮弦唱平湖调，文词数折，其音袅袅，如流莺云 - 描述了她夜晚與家人圍坐聽她彈奏阮琴並演唱平湖調的場景，其聲音柔美如黃鶯，反映了古代中國的音樂和文學活動。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妙事、妙人、妙文 - Describe things, people, and writings that are extraordinary or excellent.
  • 令观者叹赏不置 - Make the viewers unable to stop admiring, indicating a high level of appreciation or praise.
  • 幼失怙 - Lost his father at a young age. '失怙' is a term used to describe the loss of a father.
  • 入泮 - Entered the school. '泮' refers to a school or academy in ancient China.
  • 韶秀无比 - Extremely graceful and beautiful. '韶秀' describes someone who is both graceful and handsome.
  • 人美其名曰“郑大姐” - People praised him with the nickname 'Big Sister Zheng.' This suggests a unique or affectionate nickname given to Zheng due to his appearance or character.
  • 聘陈氏，未娶夭殂 - Engaged to a Chen family but died before marriage. '夭殂' refers to an untimely death, often used for young people.
  • 慎择所配 - Carefully chose his partner. '所配' refers to a spouse or partner.
  • 恐拂其意 - Fearing to go against his wishes. '拂其意' means to contradict or oppose someone's intentions.
  • 高自位置 - Highly esteemed himself. '高自位置' indicates a high self-regard or self-esteem.
  • 二十五而求凰未就也 - At twenty-five, he had not yet found a match. '求凰' is a metaphor for finding a suitable partner, often used in the context of marriage.
  • 入郡 - Entered the prefecture. '郡' refers to a prefecture or administrative region.
  • 府桥下一小绫绢铺 - A small silk and satin shop under a bridge in the prefecture. '绫绢' refers to fine silk and satin fabrics.
  • 柜前有女子白洁，瓜仁面，腰细刚一捻 - A girl stood by the counter, white and clean, with a round face like a melon seed, and a waist as thin as a pinch. '瓜仁面' describes a face round like a melon seed, and '腰细刚一捻' emphasizes her extremely slender waist.
  • 心爱好之 - He liked her very much. '心爱好' means to have a strong liking or affection.
  • 趋入铺买绫 - He hurried into the shop to buy silk. '趋入' means to hurry into.
  • 女呼其兄，兄出 - The girl called her brother, who came out. '兄' refers to her brother.
  • 非郑意，乃故为低昂而去 - Not what Zheng wanted, so he deliberately lowered his head and left. '故为低昂' means to deliberately lower one's head, indicating a polite refusal.
  • 明日郑见女，又至 - The next day, Zheng saw the girl again and went back. '又至' means to go back or return.
  • 指架上包：“即此绫。昨已言明若干镪。” - He pointed to a package on the shelf: 'This is the silk. I already told you the price yesterday.' '镪' is an old term for money.
  • 女初利其价，遂与郑 - The girl initially liked the price and agreed to Zheng. '利其价' means she found the price favorable.
  • 郑脱贯如价 - Zheng paid the price. '脱贯' means to pay.
  • 女沉吟，返其半曰：“毋须太多。” - The girl hesitated and returned half of the money, saying, 'Not too much is needed.' '沉吟' means to hesitate or ponder.
  • 郑德之，由此日往觑焉 - Zheng felt grateful and started visiting her daily. '德之' means to feel grateful, and '日往觑焉' means to visit daily.
  • 既而女亦目逆而送之 - Soon, the girl also looked after him and saw him off. '目逆而送之' means to look after and see someone off.
  • 郑乃属媒妪通其意 - Zheng then asked a matchmaker to convey his intentions. '属媒妪' means to ask a matchmaker.
  • 其兄返命曰：“弱妹得秀才耦，大佳，但只好作画中人，恐不任井臼事。” - Her brother replied: 'A young sister getting a scholar as a partner is great, but she is only fit to be a figure in a painting, and may not be able to handle household chores.' '弱妹' means a young sister, '秀才耦' means a scholar as a partner, '画中人' means a figure in a painting, and '井臼事' refers to household chores.
  • 吾家颇有薄产，断不至使新妇入厨下 - Our family has some modest property, so I won't let the new wife enter the kitchen. '薄产' means modest property, and '入厨下' means to enter the kitchen.
  • 归告其母，遂委禽焉 - He returned home and told his mother, and they finally arranged the marriage. '委禽' is an old term for arranging a marriage.

  • 辞父母曰 - She bid farewell to her parents and said.
  • 儿已为冥府王妃 - I have become the queen of the underworld.
  • 外舆从久驾 - The outer carriage has been waiting for a long time.
  • 儿不敢稽 - I dare not delay.
  • 从此侯门似海 - From now on, the gate of the noble family is like the sea, a metaphor for the difficulty of re-entering the world of the living.
  • 膝下长辞 - Farewell to my parents' knees, a traditional way of saying goodbye to one's parents.
  • 无过伤痛 - Do not grieve too much.
  • 今儿作王侯妃 - Now I am the queen of the nobles.
  • 充六宫 - Fill the six palaces, a metaphor for being a queen with many duties.
  • 班九嫔 - Rank among the nine consorts, a reference to the hierarchy of the royal harem.
  • 岂比作田舍郎媳妇 - How could it compare to being the wife of a country bumpkin.
  • 尚烦二老体恤耶 - Still trouble my parents with their concerns.
  • 乃自吟曰 - She then recited.
  • 大邦有子，遵彼海滨 - A great state has a daughter, who follows to the seaside, a poetic line.
  • 窈窕淑女，曰嫔九京 - A graceful and virtuous lady, who becomes a consort in the nine graves, where '九京' is a euphemism for the underworld.
  • 言讫而逝 - After finishing her words, she passed away.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岳庙 - The ancestral temple of the husband's family, a significant cultural institution in traditional Chinese society.
  • 转轮王殿 - Likely a reference to a specific hall in the temple, possibly named after a Buddhist deity, reflecting the influence of Buddhism in the region.
  • 冥府 - The underworld, a concept from Chinese folklore and mythology, often associated with the afterlife and the realm of the dead.
  • 六宫 - A term used to describe the six palaces in the imperial court, often used metaphorically to refer to a queen's duties.
  • 九嫔 - The nine consorts in the royal harem, a hierarchical structure in the imperial court.
  • 田舍郎 - A term for a country bumpkin or a simple farmer, often used to contrast with the nobility.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 年十五，随母诣岳庙，游两廊间 - This sequence introduces Luzhu's age, her action, and the setting, all in a single sentence.
  • 至转轮王殿，诸娣姊皆游观 - This clause describes Luzhu's arrival at the hall and the presence of her sisters-in-law.
  • 如珠手指王像曰 - This clause introduces Luzhu's action and her statement, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 如此狰狞王，夫人朝夕对之，不栗生畏怖耶 - This clause is a question posed by Luzhu, with a clear structure but a complex meaning.
  • 汝畏之，夜间即来娶汝 - This clause is a playful or ominous remark made by one of her sisters-in-law.
  • 如珠答云 - This clause introduces Luzhu's response, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 王如欲之，我何畏彼哉 - This clause is a rhetorical question posed by Luzhu, expressing her defiance.
  • 及归家，病，既乃大渐，百药罔效 - This sequence describes Luzhu's return home, her illness, and the ineffectiveness of medicine.
  • 忽自语云 - This clause introduces Luzhu's sudden self-speech, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 越三日癸丑，我当入宫，拜受采纳矣 - This clause is Luzhu's prediction of her fate, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 众以为谵 - This clause describes the reaction of those around her, who thought she was delirious.
  • 如珠令人为之薰沐，着新衣，辞父母曰 - This sequence describes Luzhu's preparation for her supposed fate, including bathing, dressing, and bidding farewell to her parents.
  • 儿已为冥府王妃 - This clause is Luzhu's announcement of her new status, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 外舆从久驾，儿不敢稽 - This clause describes the urgency of her situation, with the carriage waiting for her.
  • 从此侯门似海，膝下长辞 - This clause is Luzhu's farewell to her parents, expressing the difficulty of re-entering the world of the living.
  • 无过伤痛 - This clause is Luzhu's consoling statement to her parents, encouraging them not to grieve too much.
  • 今儿作王侯妃，充六宫，班九嫔 - This clause describes Luzhu's new status and duties, emphasizing her elevated position.
  • 岂比作田舍郎媳妇，尚烦二老体恤耶 - This clause contrasts her new status with her previous life, expressing gratitude and relief.
  • 乃自吟曰 - This clause introduces Luzhu's recitation, which is a key moment in the narrative.
  • 大邦有子，遵彼海滨 - This line is a poetic expression of her new status.
  • 窈窕淑女，曰嫔九京 - This line is a poetic expression of her new status, using a euphemism for the underworld.
  • 言讫而逝 - This clause describes Luzhu's passing.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无兄弟 - Indicates that Vang Rang had no siblings, which was unusual and likely contributed to his parents' special attention.
  • 美丰仪 - Describes Vang Rang as having a handsome appearance, emphasizing his physical attractiveness.
  • 慧 - Suggests that Vang Rang was intelligent, adding to his overall appeal.
  • 十五应童子试 - Refers to Vang Rang taking the examination for young scholars at the age of fifteen, a significant milestone in his education.
  • 过平康，见妓心荡 - Describes Vang Rang's reaction to seeing prostitutes in the pleasure district, indicating his emotional and physical response.
  • 晚潜往妓家宿 - Indicates that Vang Rang secretly went to the pleasure district to spend the night, showing his impulsiveness and lack of restraint.
  • 招覆，师觅之不得 - Suggests that the teacher had to search for Vang Rang after he disappeared, highlighting the disruption caused by his actions.
  • 将曙，让始至，考棚已封门 - Describes the situation where Vang Rang returned just as the examination hall was closing, emphasizing the urgency and consequences of his actions.
  • 师以书贻其父 - Indicates that the teacher wrote a letter to Vang Rang's father, likely to report the incident and seek guidance.
  • 儿大矣，当婚 - Suggests that Vang Rang's mother believed he was old enough to get married, reflecting the societal norms of the time.
  • 聘马氏女 - Indicates that Vang Rang was betrothed to a girl from the Ma family, setting the stage for his marriage.
  • 是年，让入学，遂婚矣 - Describes the sequence of events where Vang Rang was admitted to school and subsequently married, highlighting the rapid progression of his life.
  • 凡见庭花新摘色艳者，必手揉碎之以为快意 - Describes the bride's peculiar behavior of crushing newly picked, brightly colored flowers, indicating her emotional instability.
  • 合卺后，夫妻若胶漆 - Suggests that Vang Rang and his wife were very close and inseparable, emphasizing the strength of their relationship.
  • 马见让之溺器乌啄而长项，恶之，熔化成饼 - Describes the bride's reaction to Vang Rang's urinal, which she found repulsive and melted into a cake, highlighting her emotional and physical aversion.
  • 夜，让求之弗得，乃用女器，自此让并不敢与妻异溺器 - Indicates that Vang Rang had to use his wife's urinal, and from then on, he did not use a separate one, showing the impact of her actions on their daily life.
  • 让斋中挂一仇实父美人图，马见之辄痛心，裂之如糜，疾遂瘳 - Describes the bride's reaction to a painting of a beautiful woman, which she tore up in distress, leading to her recovery, suggesting a complex emotional response.
  • 让后不得就外寝，渐至出必告、反必面焉 - Suggests that Vang Rang was restricted in his movements, having to inform his wife before leaving and returning, indicating the increasing control she exerted over him.
  • 每有所事出，马以如意簪点胭脂印其要处，如守宫砂，归而验 - Describes the bride's practice of marking Vang Rang's body with a red mark using a hairpin, indicating her possessiveness and control.
  • 稍不符，便穷诘研问，至再至三 - Suggests that if Vang Rang did not return as expected, the bride would interrogate him repeatedly, showing her strict and controlling nature.
  • 不数年，妻之焰日以张，让之气日以馁 - Describes how the bride's dominance increased over time, while Vang Rang's confidence and spirit diminished, highlighting the power dynamics in their relationship.
  • 让愈防检，而过愈丛积，几不可支 - Suggests that Vang Rang's efforts to be cautious only led to more mistakes, making it difficult for him to cope, indicating the complexity of his situation.
  • 为翁姑者劝之，马怒曰：“汝养子不教，我为汝约束，不德我，反仇我耶？” - Describes the in-laws' attempts to mediate, but the bride's angry response, indicating her resistance to any interference, showing her strong-willed nature.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 童子试 - Refers to the examination for young scholars, a significant educational milestone in traditional Chinese society.
  • 平康 - Refers to the pleasure district, a place known for its entertainment and prostitution, reflecting the social norms and behaviors of the time.
  • 考棚 - Refers to the examination hall, a place where candidates took important exams, emphasizing the importance of education.
  • 合卺 - Refers to the wedding ceremony, a traditional Chinese ritual marking the union of a couple, highlighting the cultural significance of marriage.
  • 溺器 - Refers to a urinal, a personal item that the bride found repulsive, reflecting her emotional and physical aversion.
  • 仇实父美人图 - Refers to a painting of a beautiful woman by Qiu Ying, a famous Ming dynasty painter, indicating the cultural and artistic context of the story.
  • 守宫砂 - Refers to a red mark used to indicate fidelity, a practice in traditional Chinese culture, reflecting the societal norms around marriage and fidelity.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '明年，会乡有赛会' introduces the setting and the event.

  • The statement '恩人至矣' is the old man's recognition of the narrator.
  • The revelation '闻审之，则去岁之欠粮翁也' reveals the narrator's identity to the old man.
  • The actions '翁告其妻，延之入内' describe the old man's actions in inviting the narrator inside.
  • The initial reluctance '闻谢曰，雨住即行' shows the narrator's initial reluctance to stay.
  • The insistence '翁固挽之，闻不得已，入草舍' describes the old man's insistence and the narrator's eventual compliance.
  • The living situation '翁无他所，惟左右复室，一翁自居，一女居焉' describes the old man's living situation.
  • The expression of gratitude '翁夫妇拜跪曰，去腊蒙恩，俾得一家团聚' describes the old man and his wife's expression of gratitude.
  • The emphasis '再造深仁，铭肌浃髓' emphasizes the depth of their gratitude.
  • The description '遂指案上炉几，我两口清贫，惟日烧一炷香，祝恩人福寿增耳' describes the old man's simple life and their way of showing gratitude.
  • The humility '闻逊谢不当' shows the narrator's humility in accepting the old man's gratitude.
  • The call '翁呼曰，喜妮子，出来拜见' describes the old man calling out to his daughter to greet the narrator.

  • 兄何辞焉？固当坦腹东床耳 - Encourages the official to accept the marriage proposal.
  • 闻遂娶女，阖署称为喜娘 - The official married the daughter, and the entire office celebrated the match.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 田舍翁 - Refers to a rural elder, often used to describe a simple, honest, and kind person.
  • 香囊 - A small bag filled with fragrant herbs, often used as a gift or decoration.
  • 袜履 - Refers to socks and shoes, indicating practical and thoughtful gifts.
  • 九鼎 - In ancient China, the nine tripods were symbols of power and authority, often used to represent the weight of a decision or agreement.
  • 坦腹东床 - A metaphor for accepting a marriage proposal, derived from a story about a man who was chosen as a son-in-law because he was reclining carelessly on a couch.
  • 喜娘 - A traditional role in Chinese weddings, often played by a woman who helps with wedding preparations and celebrations.

Proper Nouns

  • 闻 - The name of the official who receives the gifts and eventually marries the daughter.
  • 单 - The name of the other official who arranges the marriage.
  • 翁 - The name of the old man who gives the gifts and offers his daughter.
  • 女 - The daughter of the old man who is eventually married to the official.
  • 尹侯 - A title or nickname for the official, indicating his position or status.
  • 阖署 - Refers to the entire office or workplace.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 東歸 - Means 'returning east,' referring to the direction of travel.
  • 展拜 - Means 'to pay respects,' indicating a formal greeting.
  • 恪順 - Means 'respectful and obedient,' describing the behavior of the person.
  • 早息不辍 - Means 'resting early but not stopping,' indicating continuous activity.
  • 代黄氏劳 - Means 'taking over Huang's work,' indicating a responsibility taken on by the person.
  • 尽委以家务 - Means 'entrusting all household affairs,' indicating a complete transfer of responsibility.
  • 董率家人 - Means 'overseeing the family,' indicating a leadership role.
  • 亲操井臼 - Means 'personally handling the grinding and pounding,' indicating hands-on involvement in household chores.
  • 课纺绩 - Means 'teaching spinning and weaving,' indicating instruction in domestic skills.
  • 功倍往昔 - Means 'achieving twice the results of the past,' indicating significant improvement.
  • 通悉田事 - Means 'thoroughly understanding agricultural matters,' indicating expertise in farming.
  • 祈晴问雨 - Means 'praying for clear weather and asking about rain,' indicating rituals related to weather.
  • 播种助苗 - Means 'planting seeds and helping the crops grow,' indicating active participation in farming.
  • 罔不井井有条 - Means 'everything is well-organized,' indicating meticulous organization.
  • 多种麦则麦收，多种黍则黍收 - Means 'planting more wheat results in more wheat harvest, planting more millet results in more millet harvest,' indicating the principle of increased yield through increased effort.
  • 虽老农圃不如也 - Means 'even experienced farmers cannot match this,' indicating exceptional skill.
  • 五六年中 - Means 'over five or six years,' indicating a specific time frame.
  • 家益裕 - Means 'the family becomes increasingly prosperous,' indicating economic growth.
  • 广治沃田四百余亩 - Means 'cultivating over four hundred acres of fertile land,' indicating extensive land management.
  • 生一子 - Means 'giving birth to one son,' indicating the birth of a child.
  • 待之如己出 - Means 'treating him as if he were her own,' indicating maternal care.
  • 不知穿食事 - Means 'not knowing how to prepare food,' indicating a lack of knowledge in basic household tasks.
  • 饭来张口，衣来动手 - Means 'opening the mouth for food and moving the hands for clothing,' indicating a life of ease and comfort.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 喜娘 - Refers to a female servant or helper, often in a household setting.
  • 闻 - Refers to Wen, likely a male figure in the story.
  • 黄氏 - Refers to Huang, likely a female figure in the story, possibly the wife of Wen.
  • 黄氏尝对人曰 - Indicates a quote from Huang, suggesting a conversation or statement she made.
  • 穿食事 - Refers to the preparation of food, a common household task.
  • 饭来张口，衣来动手 - Refers to a life of ease, where one does not have to worry about basic needs.

Proper Nouns

  • 喜娘 - The name of the maid.
  • 闻 - The name of the male figure in the story.
  • 黄氏 - The name of the female figure in the story, likely the wife of Wen.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 胳瘩老娘 - Colloquial term for an old woman, possibly with a nickname or epithet. The term '胳瘩' is not standard and may be a local or regional variant, indicating a physical characteristic or a nickname.
  • 刀笔 - Refers to the skill of writing legal documents or handling legal matters.
  • 讼师 - A lawyer or a person skilled in handling lawsuits.
  • 挽折 - To persuade or convince someone to change their mind or decision.
  • 射利 - To pursue profit or gain.
  • 计利厚则蔑理甚 - When profit is high, one may disregard reason or morality.
  • 岁不登 - A term meaning a poor harvest or famine.
  • 闭籴 - To stop buying or selling grain, often due to high prices or shortages.
  • 贩者蜂拥 - A vivid metaphor describing a large number of people rushing to buy or sell.
  • 列国分争，尚有移民移粟；天朝一统，何分江北江南 - A poetic and rhetorical statement emphasizing the contrast between the chaotic state of multiple warring states and the unified nature of a single empire, suggesting that in a unified state, regional boundaries should not matter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖州 - A city in Zhejiang Province, known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 婺妇 - A woman from the Wu region, indicating her place of origin.
  • 富甲之媳 - A wealthy man's daughter-in-law, suggesting her high social status.
  • 早孀 - A widow who lost her husband early in life.
  • 改适 - To remarry after being widowed.
  • 翁不许 - The father-in-law refusing permission, indicating the patriarchal structure of the society.
  • 强其贞守 - Forcing her to remain chaste, reflecting the societal expectations of widows.
  • 江北岁不登 - A reference to a poor harvest in the northern regions, indicating a natural disaster or economic hardship.
  • 人皆贩米江南 - People from the north traveling to the south to buy rice, highlighting the regional trade dynamics.
  • 江南之人闭籴 - People in the south refusing to sell rice, indicating market manipulation or scarcity.
  • 构讼汹汹 - A large-scale legal dispute or conflict, suggesting a widespread issue.
  • 列国分争 - A reference to the Warring States period, a time of political fragmentation and conflict.
  • 天朝一统 - The idea of a unified empire, reflecting the imperial ideology of the time.

Proper Nouns

  • 湖州 - A city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 婺妇 - A woman from the Wu region.
  • 富甲 - A wealthy man.
  • 江北 - The northern regions.
  • 江南 - The southern regions.
  • 列国 - The Warring States period.
  • 天朝 - The unified empire.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 捉刀 - Means 'to write on behalf of someone else.'
  • 立就一詞 - Means 'quickly wrote a statement.'

  • 引孟子言燕可伐一節 - Means 'cited a passage from Mencius about the possibility of attacking Yan.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 優伶 - Refers to actors or performers, often used in the context of traditional Chinese theater.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吁，是妇亦奇矣 - Exclamatory phrase meaning 'Ah, this woman is indeed extraordinary!'
  • 奈何以胳瘩名 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why is she named 'Gēdā'?' (Gēdā is a colloquial term for a bump or lump).
  • 盖亦厉气之结也 - Phrase meaning 'It seems to be a result of a malevolent energy or spirit.'
  • 天之生才，往往令人不可测有如此者 - Statement meaning 'Heaven's creation of talent is often unpredictable, as seen in such cases.'
  • 二妙 - Nickname for the girl, possibly meaning 'Two Wonders' or 'Two Beauties.'
  • 褚文兴，吴贾也 - Phrase meaning 'Zhu Wenxing is a merchant from Wu.'
  • 贸于粤，往来十易春秋 - Phrase meaning 'He traded in Guangdong, traveling back and forth for ten years.'
  • 计利倍蓰 - Phrase meaning 'He made profits several times over.'
  • 蛮烟瘴雨 - Phrase meaning 'barbaric smoke and瘴气 (miasma), referring to the harsh conditions in the southern regions.'
  • 粤有黎姓者 - Phrase meaning 'In Guangdong, there was a man surnamed Li.'
  • 褚之旧馆人也 - Phrase meaning 'He was an old acquaintance of Zhu.'
  • 多姿且慧 - Phrase meaning 'graceful and intelligent.'
  • 年十三，甫垂髫 - Phrase meaning 'at thirteen, just entering adolescence.'
  • 尝于盘鸦后束短发，缕丝作辫，披肩际 - Phrase meaning 'she would sometimes tie her hair into a short braid after the style of a young girl, with strands hanging over her shoulders.'
  • 褚每南来，多携奁具脂粉赠之 - Phrase meaning 'Whenever Zhu came south, he would often bring cosmetics and other gifts for her.'
  • 其初也，褚爱之而怜其稚 - Phrase meaning 'At first, Zhu loved her but pitied her youth.'
  • 继也，妙感之而情为移 - Phrase meaning 'Later, she felt his affection and her emotions were swayed.'
  • 逆旅之中，双环么凤，借以消遣 - Phrase meaning 'In the inn, they would play games like 'Double Rings' and 'Little Phoenix' to pass the time.'
  • 而褚亦愈久而不及乱 - Phrase meaning 'And Zhu, over time, did not act on his feelings.'
  • 会南归三载，值广南诸郡流疫，商贾断绝 - Phrase meaning 'After three years of returning south, a plague broke out in the southern regions, cutting off trade.'
  • 黎氏素无产，萧条贫惫，家遂以落 - Phrase meaning 'The Li family had no property, and their life became impoverished, leading to their decline.'
  • 二妙年十六，母死，其父鳏，尝贷为炊，日不举火 - Phrase meaning 'Er Miao was sixteen, her mother died, her father was widowed, and they often borrowed to cook, barely managing to eat.'
  • 父出不家，妙固茕茕掩双扉也 - Phrase meaning 'Her father went out and did not stay at home, leaving Er Miao alone, closing the double doors.'
  • 一日，褚忽至，黎老见之，备道苦况 - Phrase meaning 'One day, Zhu suddenly arrived, and the old man of the Li family told him about their hardships.'
  • 二妙亦羞以为容 - Phrase meaning 'Er Miao was also embarrassed to show her face.'
  • 褚不忍去而之他，仍假馆焉 - Phrase meaning 'Zhu could not bear to leave and go elsewhere, so he continued to stay in the inn.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胳瘩 - Colloquial term for a bump or lump, possibly used as a nickname.
  • 厉气 - Refers to malevolent or evil spirits or energies.
  • 二妙 - Nickname, possibly meaning 'Two Wonders' or 'Two Beauties.'
  • 褚文兴，吴贾也 - Zhu Wenxing is a merchant from Wu, a region in ancient China known for its commerce.
  • 蛮烟瘴雨 - Refers to the harsh and dangerous conditions in the southern regions of China, often associated with disease and hardship.
  • 盘鸦 - A hairstyle, possibly referring to a style worn by young girls.
  • 双环么凤 - Traditional games or pastimes, possibly board games or card games.
  • 逆旅 - Refers to an inn or temporary lodging.
  • 茕茕 - A term describing someone who is alone and helpless.

Proper Nouns

  • 褚文兴 - The name of the merchant.
  • 吴 - A region in ancient China known for its commerce.
  • 粤 - A region in southern China, known for its trade and harsh conditions.
  • 黎 - The surname of the family.
  • 二妙 - The nickname of the girl.
  • 盘鸦 - A hairstyle.
  • 双环么凤 - Traditional games or pastimes.
  • 逆旅 - An inn or temporary lodging.
  • 茕茕 - A term describing someone who is alone and helpless.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 粤有大麻疯 - Classical expression meaning 'In Guangdong there is a severe form of leprosy.'
  • 人中之，肉溃死 - Means 'When people contract it, their flesh rots and they die.'
  • 人皆屏弃，不与同巷 - Means 'People all shun them and do not let them live in the same alley.'
  • 男子不治；女有之，与人交接可疗 - Means 'Men cannot be cured; if women have it, they can be cured by sexual intercourse.'
  • 时二妙传染是疾 - Means 'At that time, the two wonders (妙) were infected with this disease.'
  • 其父使妙移于褚 - Means 'Their father made one of the wonders move to Chu (褚).'
  • 黎假出，妙至褚所 - Means 'When Li (黎) left, one of the wonders went to Chu's place.'
  • 褚喜求合，女愀然曰：“我不忍祸君也。” - Means 'Chu was happy to seek union, but the woman sadly said, 'I cannot bear to bring disaster upon you.''
  • 遂告以故，且令褚速去，并乞异日病发，望藁葬于道路之旁，言已呜咽 - Means 'She then told him the reason and asked him to leave quickly, and also begged that if she fell ill in the future, he would bury her beside the road. She wept as she spoke.'
  • 卿无悲泪！”乃出橐金贻妙，“倘果不治，卿即南来，当养卿以天年。 - Means 'You must not shed tears!' He then took out some gold from his bag and gave it to the wonder. 'If you indeed cannot be cured, come to the south, and I will support you until your natural end.'
  • 妙拜谢，褚匆匆别 - Means 'The wonder bowed in thanks, and Chu left hurriedly.'
  • 后半年创剧，溃出肌肤，众共弃之 - Means 'After half a year, her condition worsened, her skin rotted, and everyone abandoned her.'
  • 妙乃流丐而南，形益秽 - Means 'The wonder then became a beggar and moved south, her appearance becoming increasingly filthy.'
  • 十阅月至吴阊，访褚门而告 - Means 'After ten months, she reached Wu Chang and went to Chu's door to inform him.'
  • 褚收之，居以废圃 - Means 'Chu took her in and housed her in an abandoned garden.'
  • 家人日投食，皆掩鼻 - Means 'The family provided food daily, but they all covered their noses.'
  • 圃中有老槐，空其腔，蛇虺凭以为窠 - Means 'There was an old locust tree in the garden, hollowed out, which snakes and venomous creatures used as a nest.'
  • 妙食庋于牖上，蛇尝来食妙食，而妙亦食蛇所食之食 - Means 'The wonder placed her food on the window sill, and snakes often came to eat her food, and the wonder also ate the food that the snakes had eaten.'
  • 妙一日忽收脓结痂，脱然以起 - Means 'One day, the wonder suddenly recovered, her sores healed, and she rose up.'
  • 回视荐上，如败鼓皮数十片 - Means 'Looking back at the mat, there were dozens of pieces like the skins of rotten drums.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 粤 - Refers to Guangdong, a province in southern China.
  • 大麻疯 - Refers to a severe form of leprosy.
  • 二妙 - Refers to two women, likely named '妙.'
  • 褚 - A man's name, possibly a commoner.
  • 黎 - Another man's name, possibly a friend or relative.
  • 吴阊 - Refers to Wu Chang, a city in Jiangsu Province.
  • 废圃 - An abandoned garden, often used for isolation or as a place of refuge.
  • 蛇虺 - Refers to snakes and venomous creatures.
  • 藁葬 - Refers to a simple burial, often in a place where no one cares for the deceased.

Proper Nouns

  • 粤 - Guangdong.
  • 大麻疯 - Severe leprosy.
  • 二妙 - Two women.
  • 褚 - A man's name.
  • 黎 - A man's name.
  • 吴阊 - Wu Chang.
  • 废圃 - Abandoned garden.
  • 蛇虺 - Snakes and venomous creatures.
  • 藁葬 - Simple burial.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家人異之 - The family found it strange; '異' means to find something strange or unusual.
  • 如剥瓠 - Metaphor for carefully examining or scrutinizing something.
  • 居然 - Sudden or unexpected; '居然佳丽' means she suddenly became a beauty.
  • 納為姬 - Adopted as a concubine; '姬' refers to a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 葺圃 - Repairing or tending to a garden.
  • 海舶估 - Maritime trade.
  • 引鹾商 - Salt merchant.
  • 聲技 - Musical and performance arts.
  • 彈吹 - Playing stringed instruments and blowing wind instruments.
  • 消魂橋 - Metaphor for a place of intense pleasure.
  • 煩渴胥蠲 - All desires were satisfied.
  • 形似 - Resemble.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 家人異之 - Reflects the importance of family dynamics in classical Chinese society.
  • 如剥瓠 - Common metaphor in classical Chinese literature for careful examination.
  • 納為姬 - Practice of having concubines was common among the wealthy and powerful in ancient China.
  • 葺圃 - Gardens were important in classical Chinese culture, symbolizing harmony and beauty.
  • 海舶估 - Significant economic activity in ancient China.
  • 酷好聲技 - Music and performance arts were highly valued in classical Chinese culture.
  • 多姬妾，悉善彈吹 - Sign of wealth and status to have skilled concubines.
  • 門伙某自晉來 - Mention of Jin (a state in ancient China) adds historical context.
  • 年十三，發垂髫而黝黑可照，眉目如水 - Description of the maid's appearance reflects the aesthetic standards of the time.
  • 房帏間嬌容緩步，對之如在消魂橋上，煩渴胥蠲 - Poetic way to describe intense pleasure and satisfaction.
  • 群婢效之，終莫得其形似 - Unique qualities of the maid reflected in the inability of others to imitate her.

Proper Nouns

  • 家人 - The family.
  • 褚 - Chu, a character in the story.
  • 侯文智 - Hou Wenzhi, the main character.
  • 門伙 - A servant.
  • 晉 - Jin, a state in ancient China.
  • 颠當 - DianDang, the name of the maid.

[278]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 佳人一顾，可以倾城 - A well-known idiom meaning 'a glance from a beautiful woman can topple a city,' often used to describe the irresistible charm of a woman.
  • 金屋成而玉山颓矣 - A metaphorical expression where '金屋' (golden house) symbolizes wealth or status, and '玉山颓' (the jade mountain collapses) symbolizes a sudden downfall or ruin.
  • 商欠累积，一败涂地 - Describes accumulating business debts leading to complete ruin.
  • 颠当相依不去 - Means 'Dian Dang stayed by his side,' indicating loyalty or attachment.
  • 祸水也，此天遣以祸君家者也 - Dian Dang refers to herself as a 'disaster' sent by heaven to bring misfortune to the family.
  • 妾见君生平虽贪声技，蓄姬妾，尚少淫恶 - A self-assessment by Dian Dang, noting that although the man had many concubines, he was not excessively immoral.
  • 妾今娠五月，或得一子以延侯氏后 - Dian Dang says she is five months pregnant and hopes to give birth to a child to continue the Hou family line.
  • 妾祸水也，此天遣以祸君家者也 - Dian Dang repeats her self-description as a 'disaster' sent by heaven.
  • 侯家尚有旧楼，我独不能效绿珠碎首耶 - Dian Dang refers to the old mansion of the Hou family and mentions the story of Green Pearl (绿珠), a concubine who committed suicide to avoid dishonor.
  • 颠当骂曰 - Dian Dang scolds, indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 突黔而釜未生尘 - Describes a condition where the chimney is blackened but the pot has not accumulated dust, suggesting a state of neglect or abandonment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 佳人一顾，可以倾城 - Refers to the famous beauty Xi Shi and the story of her causing the fall of the state of Yue by her beauty.
  • 晋文公 - Refers to Duke Wen of Jin, a famous ruler of the Spring and Autumn period known for his wisdom and leadership.
  • 石季伦 - Refers to Shi Chong, a wealthy and debauched nobleman of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 绿珠 - A concubine of Shi Chong who committed suicide to avoid being taken by another powerful man, Sima Zhao.
  • 侯氏 - Refers to the family name of the protagonist, indicating a noble or wealthy background.
  • 商欠 - Refers to commercial debts, a common issue in ancient Chinese society.
  • 姬妾 - Refers to concubines, a common practice among the nobility and wealthy in ancient China.
  • 旧楼 - Refers to the old mansion, a symbol of the family's past wealth and status.
  • 突黔而釜未生尘 - Refers to a state of neglect, where the house is abandoned and not used.

Proper Nouns

  • 佳人 - A beautiful woman.

  • The clause '但勤针黹' is a bit ambiguous, but it can be read as a description of her practical skills.

  • The clause '故劳人思妇之作，大抵皆不得志之所为' is a bit ambiguous, but it can be read as a description of the emotional depth of poetry.
  • The clause '其感喟不平，根于心者，悉露于言' is a bit ambiguous, but it can be read as a description of the emotional depth of poetry.
  • The clause '而坎坷丛集，富于文者益穷其遇' is a bit ambiguous, but it can be read as a description of the relationship between hardship and literary achievement.
  • The clause '况内仪志美，中馈称贤，更非丈夫可比' is a bit ambiguous, but it can be read as a description of the superiority of women in certain virtues.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先去 - In this context, it likely refers to the wife's death, a common theme in classical Chinese literature where the wife often dies before the husband.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '初则妆台诘屈，床笫咿唔，口讲指画，循循善诱' describes the early stages of learning, with the teacher using various methods to guide the student.
  • The metaphor '春风座上，俨列巫山' describes the teacher's gentle and persuasive manner.
  • The sequence '日变焉，月化焉，循其序不躐其等' describes the continuous improvement and steady progress in learning.
  • The metaphors '废书三叹，顿转于秋水之流波；把卷沉吟，忽悟于樱桃之启齿' describe moments of deep reflection and sudden realization.
  • The phrase '触色闻声，罔不惬心而莫逆' describes the harmonious interaction between the teacher and student.
  • The sequence '一时之交相酬对，正静不佻，觉美而益增其艳，正妙而莫可名言' describes the mutual response and the beauty of the interaction.
  • The phrase '十易寒暑，女子呼段而进之曰' indicates a long period of time and the teacher's final advice to the student.

'梧桐叶落' means the osmanthus leaves fall, '青皮光棍' refers to a young man in his prime, '打秋风' is a colloquial expression meaning to seek to take advantage of someone.

  • 赤叶枫飘晚照残 - Red maple leaves falling and the setting sun, suggesting the end of the day and a sense of melancholy.
  • 行断雁迷云黯黯 - The journey ends, geese lost in dark clouds, indicating a sense of disorientation and loss.
  • 梦多人阻水漫漫 - Many dreams but obstacles in the water, suggesting unfulfilled desires and barriers.
  • 伤神吊影空思忆 - Sorrowful and lonely, thinking of the past, emphasizing the depth of emotional pain.
  • 凉月晶悬映彻看 - Cool moon hanging, clearly visible, creating a serene and clear image.
  • 生读罢，知为妻所作，遂什袭珍藏之 - The man reads it and realizes it was written by his wife, so he carefully preserves it, showing his appreciation and respect.
  • 女笑曰：“姊夫将醋姐物视同白玉，恐人以为砆也。” - The woman laughs and says, 'My sister-in-law treats her things like white jade, but people might think it's just a stone,' indicating a playful and slightly sarcastic tone.
  • 今日无事，与姊夫击蒙小叶子格戏，负则打掌心 - Today, there's nothing to do, so let's play a game of tiles, if I lose, I'll slap my palm, showing a light-hearted and playful mood.
  • 先是生负，女批之 - At first, the man lost, so the woman slapped him, indicating a playful and teasing interaction.
  • 忽生击得双叶，生狂喜，遂欲批女掌 - Suddenly, the man won two tiles, he was extremely happy, and wanted to slap the woman's palm, showing a reversal of roles and continued playfulness.
  • 女笑以手缩袖中不出，生固捉之 - The woman laughed and hid her hand in her sleeve, but the man insisted on catching it, indicating a playful and teasing interaction.
  • 生见指葱如而腕藕若，遂承之以口，曰：“吾欲食西子臂耳。” - The man saw her fingers like green onions and her wrists like lotus roots, so he kissed her hand and said, 'I want to eat Xi Shi's arm,' indicating a romantic and playful gesture.
  • 女急缩手，生抱求欢 - The woman quickly pulled her hand back, and the man hugged her to make love, showing a shift from playfulness to intimacy.
  • 女不得已，遂与之合 - The woman, unable to refuse, finally agreed to join him, indicating a mutual understanding and agreement.
  • 生亦备极温存，十分亲爱 - The man was also very gentle and loving, showing a deep affection and care.
  • 既而浃席流丹，娇红似染 - Afterward, they made love, and her cheeks were flushed red, like they had been dyed, creating a vivid image of passion and intimacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红灯 - Red lamp, often used in classical Chinese poetry to symbolize warmth and comfort.
  • 翠袂 - Green sleeves, a metaphor for hands, often used to describe delicate and graceful movements.
  • 黄花菊 - Yellow chrysanthemums, a symbol of autumn and often associated with loneliness and melancholy.
  • 赤叶枫 - Red maple leaves, a symbol of the changing seasons and often associated with the end of the year.
  • 西子 - Xi Shi, one of the Four Great Beauties of ancient China, known for her beauty and often used as a metaphor for beauty in literature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 往往不自检点 - Phrase meaning 'often fails to be cautious or self-disciplined,' indicating a lack of restraint or care.
  • 生时悚惕 - Means 'at the time of birth, trembling with fear,' suggesting a state of anxiety or fear.
  • 惟恐翁之知也 - Means 'only fearing that the father-in-law would know,' indicating a concern about being discovered.
  • 女告生曰 - Means 'the woman told the man,' indicating a direct speech from the woman to the man.
  • 我早孤 - Means 'I was orphaned at a young age,' indicating a personal history of loss.
  • 叔抚我，最所钟爱 - Means 'my uncle raised me, and he loved me most dearly,' indicating a close relationship and affection.
  • 谋之当无不从 - Means 'if we plan it, there will be no refusal,' indicating a high level of trust and support.
  • 我既姊也，而又妹之 - Means 'I am already your sister-in-law, and now I am also your younger sister,' indicating a complex familial relationship.
  • 恐事不谐 - Means 'I fear that the matter will not work out,' indicating a concern about the success of the plan.
  • 女于是病而不起 - Means 'the woman then fell ill and did not get up,' indicating a physical or emotional state.
  • 翁忧之 - Means 'the father-in-law was worried about her,' indicating a concern for her well-being.
  • 问女，不答 - Means 'he asked the woman, but she did not answer,' indicating a refusal to communicate.
  • 复问生，生跽自首 - Means 'he then asked the man, who knelt down and confessed,' indicating a formal act of confession.
  • 翁怒曰 - Means 'the father-in-law angrily said,' indicating a strong emotional reaction.
  • 得陇又望蜀也 - Proverb meaning 'having obtained one thing, one desires another,' indicating greed or insatiable desires.
  • 愤愤入内 - Means 'angrily went inside,' indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 见女呻吟床笫 - Means 'saw the woman moaning in bed,' indicating a state of distress.
  • 生复跽 - Means 'the man then knelt down again,' indicating a repeated act of submission.
  • 翁挽之曰 - Means 'the father-in-law held him back and said,' indicating an attempt to calm him down.
  • 非坦之罪也 - Means 'it is not your fault,' indicating an attempt to absolve the man of blame.
  • 始我揖盗开门 - Means 'at first I welcomed a thief into my house,' indicating a metaphorical act of inviting trouble.
  • 今已成舟刻木 - Means 'now it is like a boat carved from wood,' indicating a situation that is already set in motion and cannot be undone.
  • 将罪坦则小女忧，小女忧则大女辱 - Means 'if I blame you, the younger daughter will be worried, and if she is worried, the older daughter will be disgraced,' indicating a chain of negative consequences.
  • 使一坦获戾，两女失所 - Means 'if one of you is punished, both daughters will lose their place,' indicating a broader impact on the family.
  • 我必不忍 - Means 'I would never do that,' indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 迫我以不得不从之势也 - Means 'you are forcing me into a situation where I have no choice but to comply,' indicating a sense of coercion.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 《典》稱釐降 - Refers to the bestowal or descent described in the *Shangshu* (Book of Documents).
  • 《風》咏饯郊 - Refers to the farewell ceremony described in the *Shijing* (Book of Songs).
  • 洵两美以同妍，自双葩以并秀 - Uses the metaphor of two beautiful flowers to describe a harmonious union.
  • 兹者再结麟文之彩，重联凤喙之胶 - Describes a reconnection using the metaphors of the qilin and the phoenix.
  • 二薛联姻，竟是今朝永叔；小乔初嫁，应知昔日周郎 - Refers to the marriages of the Xue family and the historical figures Ouyang Xiu and Zhou Yu.
  • 旧女婿为新女婿，半子之分当兼；小姨夫是大姨夫，两大之间并重 - Describes the relationships between in-laws and the importance of family ties.
  • 当年鹊驾，宁先入者称尊；此际鸾栖，岂后来者居上 - Uses the metaphors of the magpie and the phoenix to describe social hierarchy and respect.
  • 本是同心树，弟不先兄；原为并蒂花，姐犹似娣 - Uses the metaphors of a tree of the same heart and a twin-flowered plant to describe family harmony.
  • 更信人行暮雨，看镜里之双栖；何妨婢唤春风，拟溪边之三笑 - Uses the metaphors of walking in the evening rain and calling the spring wind to describe a romantic scene.
  • 将左宜者自符右有，无后轻者愈少前轩 - Uses the metaphor of the left and right to describe the importance of following and leading.
  • 爰赋联芳，永偕合璧 - Uses the metaphor of linked fragrance and paired jade disks to describe a lasting union.
  • 映彩车于户外，雅照三星；挹绣羽于堂前，巧逢双燕矣 - Uses the metaphors of a colorful carriage and embroidered feathers to describe a festive scene.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 览毕喜 - Literally 'looked over and was delighted,' indicating a positive reaction after reading or examining something.
  • 遂令佾娘与生成婚 - 'Thus ordered the maiden Yi to marry the son Cheng,' where '遂' indicates a result or consequence.
  • 捷南宫 - 'Passed the imperial examination,' a term specific to the Chinese civil service examination system.
  • 入词垣 - 'Entered the Hanlin Academy,' referring to a prestigious position in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 假归省墓 - 'Returned home on leave to visit the ancestral graves,' a common practice for officials to pay respects to their ancestors.
  • 营旧居数处 - 'Set up several old residences,' suggesting the family has multiple properties or homes.
  • 家人亲串如蚁 - 'Family members and relatives were as numerous as ants,' a vivid metaphor for a large gathering.
  • 披衣启扉 - 'Put on clothes and opened the door,' a formal action indicating respect or urgency.
  • 贱婢 - 'Wretched maid,' a derogatory term used by one character to insult another.
  • 谁不是一个汉 - 'Who isn't a man?' a colloquial expression implying that everyone is human and should be treated equally.
  • 孤影对四壁 - 'Alone with one's shadow facing four walls,' a metaphor for loneliness and isolation.
  • 床上接踵 - 'People sitting on the bed one after another,' a metaphor for overcrowding or invasion of personal space.
  • 从何处得信来 - 'How did you get the news?' a question asking about the source of information.
  • 咽声，振衣欲去 - 'Sobbing, she shook her clothes and was about to leave,' a vivid description of emotional distress and the act of leaving.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南宫 - The Imperial Examination Hall, where the highest level of the civil service examination was held.
  • 词垣 - The Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution in the imperial bureaucracy responsible for drafting important documents and providing counsel to the emperor.
  • 佾娘 - A maiden named Yi, likely a courtesan or a woman of high social standing.
  • 醋娘 - A maiden named Su, possibly a rival or a former lover.
  • 叔父陷人 - 'Uncle has entrapped me,' suggesting a family member has caused a problem or placed someone in a difficult situation.
  • 返我外母家 - 'Return to my mother's family,' a reference to going back to one's maternal relatives, often seen as a last resort in family conflicts.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惶恐 - Expresses extreme fear or anxiety.
  • 两处拜揖哀恳 - Describes the act of bowing and pleading earnestly in two places, indicating a formal and sincere gesture.
  • 前言戏耳 - Means 'what I said before was just a joke,' indicating that previous statements were not to be taken seriously.
  • 徒负有醋之名，而无醋之实 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that someone has a reputation for jealousy (醋, 'vinegar') but lacks the substance of jealousy.
  • 忍而为此态耳 - Means 'I was just pretending to be this way,' implying that the previous behavior was feigned.
  • 破涕为笑 - Describes the act of stopping crying and starting to laugh, indicating a shift from sadness to joy.
  • 愿夫人有虚名而无实践也 - A polite request that the woman has only a reputation for jealousy without actually being jealous.
  • 认父 - Means 'to acknowledge a father,' typically in the context of a child recognizing their father.
  • 问外母安 - Means 'to ask after the mother-in-law,' a polite inquiry about the well-being of the mother-in-law.
  • 问岳翁近履 - Means 'to ask about the father-in-law's recent activities,' a polite inquiry about the father-in-law's recent well-being.
  • 入晋 - Means 'to enter Shanxi,' indicating a trip or move to the region of Shanxi.
  • 常至生家探二女 - Means 'often visits the son-in-law's home to see the two daughters,' indicating frequent visits to the son-in-law's house.
  • 得房中之乐 - Means 'to enjoy the pleasures of the bedroom,' indicating marital happiness.
  • 不愿利达 - Means 'does not wish for wealth and success,' indicating a preference for a simple life.
  • 适意林泉 - Means 'to find pleasure in the mountains and waters,' indicating a preference for a peaceful, natural lifestyle.
  • 闭门谢客 - Means 'to close the door and refuse visitors,' indicating a desire for solitude and privacy.
  • 诙谐诗酒 - Describes a lighthearted and playful atmosphere involving poetry and wine.
  • 瀹茗敲棋 - Describes the act of brewing tea and playing chess, indicating a leisurely and intellectual activity.
  • 唱和颇多 - Means 'there are many exchanges of poetry,' indicating frequent poetic interactions.
  • 有《漉酿集》诗 - Refers to a collection of poems titled *Lü Nang Ji* (漉酿集), suggesting a literary work.
  • 惜未梓 - Means 'unfortunately, it has not been printed,' indicating that the collection remains unpublished.
  • 尝见其四绝云 - Means 'I have seen four of his absolute best works,' indicating a reference to particularly outstanding poems.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 林泉 - Refers to mountains and waters, symbolizing a peaceful and natural lifestyle.
  • 闭门谢客 - Refers to a practice of secluding oneself from the outside world, often associated with scholars and recluses.
  • 诙谐诗酒 - Refers to a literary and social activity involving poetry and wine, a common pastime among literati.
  • 瀹茗敲棋 - Refers to the leisurely activities of brewing tea and playing chess, indicating a refined and intellectual lifestyle.
  • 唱和颇多 - Refers to the practice of exchanging poems, a common literary activity in classical Chinese culture.
  • 《漉酿集》 - Refers to a specific collection of poems, indicating a literary work.
  • 四绝 - Refers to the four best works, indicating a high level of literary achievement.

  • 佾姑 - Refers to a woman, likely the wife of the protagonist.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一双金菊对芙蓉 - Poetic description, possibly metaphorical, but its exact meaning is unclear without further context.
  • 取次风流在个中 - 取次 can mean 'casually' or 'unintentionally', while 风流 can refer to elegance or charm. The phrase suggests that elegance or charm naturally arises in this context.
  • 恰似鱼游莲叶底 - Simile comparing something to a fish swimming under a lotus leaf, possibly symbolizing grace or ease.
  • 刚从西去又还东 - Metaphor for something that goes in one direction but returns, possibly symbolizing change or impermanence.
  • 亚字栏中花两枝 - 亚字栏 refers to a specific type of lattice or fence, and 花两枝 means 'two branches of flowers'.
  • 娇含嫩蕊未开时 - Describes flowers that are still closed, with delicate buds.
  • 东君着意和香摘 - 东君 is a poetic term for the god of spring, and 着意 means 'intentionally' or 'carefully'. The phrase suggests that the spring god carefully picks flowers with fragrance.
  • 不使无端蜂蝶知 - Means 'not allowing the bees and butterflies to know', possibly implying that the flowers are kept hidden or protected.
  • 一边送暖一边寒 - Metaphor for something that brings both warmth and cold, possibly symbolizing duality or contrast.
  • 二女同居志也安 - 二女同居 refers to two women living together, and 志也安 means 'their hearts are at peace'.
  • 自是联辉兰蕙好 - 联辉 means 'linked brilliance', and 兰蕙 refers to orchids and sweet-scented grasses, symbolizing elegance. The phrase suggests that the brilliance of orchids and sweet-scented grasses is naturally good.
  • 不教左右做人难 - 左右 can mean 'both sides' or 'those around', and 做人难 means 'it is difficult to be a person'. The phrase suggests that the situation does not make it difficult for those around to live their lives.
  • 川字烟儿品字茶 - 川字 and 品字 are specific patterns or arrangements, possibly referring to the way tea is arranged or served.
  • 鼎称恩爱总无差 - 鼎称 means 'equally balanced', and 恩爱 refers to love and affection. The phrase suggests that love and affection are always in perfect balance.
  • 乘鸾合在三株树 - 乘鸾 means 'riding a phoenix', a symbol of auspiciousness, and 三株树 refers to three trees. The phrase suggests that riding a phoenix is in harmony with three trees.
  • 化雪还同六出花 - 化雪 means 'snow melting', and 六出花 refers to snowflakes, which have six points. The phrase suggests that melting snow is like six-petaled flowers.
  • 生每问二女命名之义 - 生 refers to the person asking, 二女 means 'two women', and 命名之义 means 'the meaning of their names'. The phrase suggests that the person often asks about the meanings of the two women's names.
  • 醋字，以女生之日时 - 醋字 is a name, and 以女生之日时 means 'based on the day and time of her birth'.
  • 佾字，以女生之月也 - 佾字 is another name, and 以女生之月也 means 'based on the month of her birth'.
  • 后生寿八十，无疾终 - 后生 means 'the person who was born later', 寿八十 means 'lived to eighty', and 无疾终 means 'died without illness'.
  • 生终身未尝问二女为何物也 - 生 refers to the person, 终身 means 'throughout his life', 未尝 means 'never', and 问二女为何物也 means 'asked what the two women were'.
  • 二女亦同是日死 - 二女 means 'the two women', 亦 means 'also', and 同是日死 means 'died on the same day'.
  • 合葬日，女柩皆空 - 合葬日 means 'the day of joint burial', and 女柩皆空 means 'the coffins of the women were empty'.
  • 其子孙皆科第相望 - 其子孙 means 'their descendants', 皆 means 'all', 科第 refers to passing the imperial examinations, and 相望 means 'in succession'.

  • 我当遣人觅十洲同我往泗也 - '遣人' means 'send someone,' '觅' means 'to find,' and '同我往泗' means 'to accompany me to Sizhou.' Indicates that Xu will send someone to find Ten Isles to accompany him to Sizhou.
  • 君后当发 - '君' is a respectful term for the addressee, '后' means 'later,' and '当发' means 'will be promoted.' Means that the addressee will be promoted later.

  • 此天定之数，不可逃也 - '此' means 'this,' '天定之数' means 'fate determined by heaven,' and '不可逃也' means 'cannot be avoided.' Means that this is fate determined by heaven and cannot be avoided.
  • 每一念及，吊影伤情 - '每一念及' means 'every time I think of it,' '吊影' means 'to console one's shadow,' and '伤情' means 'to feel sorrow.' Conveys that every time the addressee thinks of it, he consoles his shadow and feels sorrow.
  • 未尝不嗟悼于室迩人遐也 - '未尝' means 'never,' '嗟悼' means 'to lament,' and '室迩人遐' means 'close in distance but far in relationship.' Means that the addressee never fails to lament the closeness in distance but the distance in relationship.
