  • 完璧归赵，犹反手也 - 完璧归赵 is a metaphor for returning something intact, and 犹反手也 means 'as easy as turning one's hand.' This phrase suggests that returning something intact is very easy.
  • 负心人殆以我为孤注耳 - 负心人 means 'a faithless person,' 殆 means 'probably,' 以我为孤注耳 means 'considering me as a last bet.' This phrase suggests that the faithless person sees the woman as a last bet.
  • 今何望矣 - 今 means 'now,' 何望矣 means 'what hope is there.' This phrase expresses a sense of despair.

  • 彼实负我，非忍相负也 - 彼 means 'he,' 实负我 means 'truly betrayed me,' 非忍相负也 means 'not out of cruelty but betrayal.' This phrase clarifies that the betrayal was not out of cruelty.
  • 张知尹在生时嗜赌，产荡尽 - 张知尹 is a proper name, 在生时 means 'in his lifetime,' 嗜赌 means 'loved gambling,' 产荡尽 means 'his property was depleted.' This phrase describes the man's love for gambling and the depletion of his property.
  • 后为博徒所困，陷以滚赌，冤死狱中 - 后 means 'later,' 为博徒所困 means 'was trapped by gamblers,' 陷以滚赌 means 'forced into a rolling bet,' 冤死狱中 means 'died unjustly in prison.' This phrase describes the man's subsequent troubles and death.

  • 亦可哀也 - 亦 means 'also,' 可哀也 means 'pitiful.' This phrase expresses pity.
  • 行十馀里，至镇上社祠前 - 行十馀里 means 'walked more than ten miles,' 至镇上社祠前 means 'to the front of the village temple.' This phrase describes the journey.
  • 役入内，良久出 - 役 means 'subordinate,' 入内 means 'went inside,' 良久出 means 'came out after a long time.' This phrase describes the subordinate going inside and coming out after a long time.

  • 石哀之 - 石 is a proper name, 哀之 means 'pitied her.' This phrase describes the man's pity.

  • 殡葬后仍携眷而东 - Means 'after the funeral, he still took his family eastward,' indicating continued travel after a significant loss.

  • 此姊去妹，三年孤苦 - Means 'this sister parted from her sister, enduring three years of loneliness.'
  • 離父母，會張郎 - Means 'away from her parents, meeting Zhang Lang (Mr. Zhang).'
  • 郁郁於此 - Means 'suffering in this place.'
  • 今復何時 - Means 'now what time is it?' or 'now what has become of us?'
  • 樹猶如此，不禁令人悲喜交集耳 - Means 'the tree has grown so much, it can't help but evoke a mix of joy and sorrow.'
  • 各生一子，視同己出 - Means 'each gave birth to a son, treating them as their own.'
  • 張官至和州牧，卒於署 - Means 'Zhang became the governor of Hezhou and died in his office.'
  • 夫人命其子詣杭，扶櫬來東，皆合葬焉 - Means 'the lady ordered her son to go to Hangzhou, carry the coffin eastward, and bury them together.'
  • 慨自南齊衰世，東昏驕淫 - Means 'reflecting on the declining era of the Southern Qi dynasty, with the emperor's arrogance and debauchery.'
  • 縱一日之侈靡，贻後人之沿襲 - Means 'allowing one day of extravagance to be passed down to future generations as a model.'
  • 金蓮貼地 - Refers to a practice of walking with the toes touching the ground, symbolizing femininity and delicacy.
  • 浸假而閨房士女，無不學步後塵 - Means 'gradually, women in the inner chambers all followed suit.'
  • 亡國之習，流毒一至於此 - Means 'the customs of a fallen state have spread to this extent.'
  • 吁，始作俑者，其無後乎 - Means 'alas, the one who first started this practice, will he have no descendants?'
  • 然而風俗於人，賢者不免，又烏能力矯其眾非，而一衷於獨是 - Means 'however, customs affect even the virtuous, and how can one correct the many wrongs and be alone in being right?'
  • 此馬二娘之自愛其纖纖也，可述已 - Means 'this is the story of Ma Erniang's self-love for her delicate nature, which can be told.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一双么凤巧如锥 - Metaphorical expression describing something as delicate and pointed, like a needle. '么凤' (small phoenix) is a poetic term often used to describe delicate or beautiful things.
  • 小立翘然恐不支 - Vivid description of something standing upright but appearing fragile or unstable, as if it might not support itself.
  • 春暖瓣开花绰约 - Poetic phrase describing the blooming of flowers in spring, with '绰约' (graceful and elegant) emphasizing the beauty of the blossoms.
  • 夜深钩上月参差 - Metaphorical description of the moon's appearance at night, with '参差' (uneven or irregular) suggesting the moon's phases or its reflection in water.
  • 脱来似剥新菱角 - Simile comparing the act of removing something to peeling a fresh water chestnut, suggesting delicacy and care.
  • 裹后如缠嫩笋皮 - Simile comparing the act of wrapping something to wrapping it in the tender skin of a young bamboo shoot, emphasizing delicacy and freshness.
  • 漫拟凌波仙浦外 - Poetic phrase suggesting a comparison to a fairy-like figure standing on water, with '凌波' (walking on water) and '仙浦' (fairy pond) evoking a dreamlike or otherworldly scene.
  • 轻盈好向掌中持 - Descriptive phrase emphasizing the lightness and delicacy of something, suggesting it can be easily held in the palm of one's hand.
  • 曾向潘妃步后尘 - Historical allusion to the beautiful and graceful steps of Pan's wife, a reference to the elegance and refinement of ancient Chinese nobility.
  • 弯来寸寸可堪珍 - Poetic phrase emphasizing the value and delicacy of something, suggesting that every inch of it is precious.
  • 印成软玉留香径 - Metaphorical description of something leaving a soft, fragrant trail, like a path made of soft jade.
  • 舞罢轻莲落绣茵 - Poetic phrase describing the graceful movement of something, like a lotus flower gently falling onto an embroidered carpet.
  • 怕是蹴醒春梦客 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that something might wake someone from a spring dream, with '蹴醒' (kicking awake) adding a playful or unexpected element.
  • 几回勾动早朝人 - Poetic phrase suggesting that something has stirred or aroused the early risers, with '勾动' (stirring) emphasizing the subtle influence.
  • 深闺夜静双遗舄 - Poetic phrase describing a quiet night in a secluded room, with '遗舄' (abandoned shoes) suggesting the absence of someone.
  • 还当金钱问卜频 - Metaphorical expression suggesting the frequent use of divination or fortune-telling, with '金钱问卜' (using money to ask for a fortune) emphasizing the importance placed on such practices.
  • 女敛衽曰 - Formal and respectful way of saying 'the woman bowed her sleeves and said,' indicating a polite and deferential manner of speaking.
  • 得此佳韵，死有馀荣矣 - Poetic phrase expressing deep satisfaction and honor, suggesting that even in death, one would feel proud.
  • 乃缅述其为鬼，并生前遭际坎坷 - Narrative phrase describing the recounting of a ghost's story, including the hardships and difficulties faced in life.
  • 声泪俱迸 - Vivid description of crying and sobbing, with '声泪俱迸' (both sound and tears bursting forth) emphasizing the intensity of the emotion.
  • 卿何不效唐张云容故事乎 - Rhetorical question asking why the woman does not follow the example of Zhang Yunrong from the Tang dynasty, suggesting a historical precedent for her actions.
  • 游魂所变，半涉荒幻 - Poetic phrase describing the transformation of a wandering soul, with '半涉荒幻' (partially involving the fantastical) emphasizing the otherworldly nature of the change.
  • 即令复起，无能益我，适足祸人，不屑为也 - Philosophical statement expressing the belief that even if the soul were to return, it would not benefit the person and might instead bring harm, leading to a rejection of such a possibility.
  • 言罢，杳然成烟，氤氲于衽席间，经时而散 - Poetic description of the woman's departure, with '杳然成烟' (vanishing into thin air) and '氤氲于衽席间' (fading away in the space between the bedcovers) emphasizing the ethereal and transient nature of her presence.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 济宁有刘姓者 - Common Classical Chinese construction meaning 'There was a man of the Liu surname in Jining.'
  • 为吾友王惺斋砚席交 - Literally 'was a close friend of my friend Wang Xingzai, a fellow scholar and friend at the writing desk.' Indicates a close intellectual relationship.
  • 性悭吝，有半伊尹之风 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'He was stingy, with half the virtue of Yi Yin.' Yi Yin was a famous minister in ancient China known for his wisdom and virtue.
  • 负人债多不还 - Literally 'owed many debts to others but did not repay them.'
  • 又有富岁子弟之行 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'He also behaved like a spoiled rich kid.' Suggests a spoiled and irresponsible attitude.
  • 一日，为冥司勾去 - Common phrase in Classical Chinese meaning 'One day, he was summoned by the underworld officials.'
  • 见冥官 - Literally 'met the underworld officials.'
  • 官怒曰 - Literally 'the official angrily said.'
  • 负人债务累累，是设心不偿还耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Owing so many debts, does this mean you have no intention of repaying them?'
  • 笞二十 - Common punishment in ancient China meaning 'He was flogged twenty times.'
  • 而醒告人曰 - Literally 'and when he woke up, he told people.'
  • 笞之官戴亮蓝顶，见其举手掣签时，亦尖口袍袖云 - Detailed description of the underworld official's appearance and actions. '笞之官' means 'the flogging official,' '戴亮蓝顶' means 'wearing a bright blue hat,' '举手掣签' means 'raising his hand to draw a lot,' and '尖口袍袖云' means 'his mouth and sleeves were sharp and pointed.'
  • 孟氏家鬼 - Title indicating the story about the ghost in the Meng family.
  • 邹邑孟氏，贤裔家 - Literally 'The Meng family in Zou County, a family of virtuous descendants.'
  • 长房多绝，又相继夭殂 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'The eldest branch of the family had many deaths, and they died one after another.'
  • 皆支嗣，家多孀妇 - Literally 'All were collateral descendants, and the family had many widows.'
  • 前厅厝三世柩，未就窀穸 - Detailed description of the family's situation. '前厅厝三世柩' means 'Three generations of coffins were placed in the front hall,' and '未就窀穸' means 'not yet buried in a proper tomb.'
  • 余从兄雨亭系其内戚 - Literally 'My cousin Yu Ting is related to them by marriage.'
  • 尝仆马往探，晚则宿焉 - Literally 'I once went to visit them on horseback, and stayed there at night.'
  • 夜谈，止其家，遣婢灯送书舍宿 - Literally 'At night, I talked with them, stayed at their home, and a maid lit the way to the study where I slept.'
  • 舍则前厅西厢 - Literally 'The study was in the west wing of the front hall.'
  • 婢入内，阖宅门 - Literally 'The maid went inside, and closed the main gate.'
  • 前厅去大门守宿处甚远 - Literally 'The front hall was far from the main gate where the guards were stationed.'
  • 呼其仆，不知所往 - Literally 'I called for his servant, but I didn't know where he went.'
  • 初料其事刍秣，备戒旦行 - Literally 'I initially thought he was taking care of the horses' feed, preparing to leave early the next day.'
  • 雨亭素有胆气，亦不介意 - Literally 'Yu Ting has always had courage, and he didn't mind it.'
  • 出院中，徘徊阶除间 - Literally 'He walked out of the courtyard, pacing back and forth in the hall.'
  • 明月横空，寒云四起，颇有苍凉之况 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'The bright moon hung in the sky, and cold clouds gathered all around, creating a rather desolate atmosphere.'
  • 第见一庭如水，壮志顿消，有不禁惕惕然为之心恻者也 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'Only seeing the courtyard like water, his heroic aspirations were suddenly extinguished, and he couldn't help but feel a sense of unease and sorrow.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 入舍闭管 - Literally 'enter the room and close the pipe,' likely referring to closing the windows or doors to prevent drafts or intrusions.
  • 就东北榻 - Means 'go to the bed in the northeast corner.'
  • 榻临窗，皆疏棂 - Describes a bed near the window with latticed shutters.
  • 几上灯膏殆尽 - Means 'the oil in the lamp on the table is almost gone.'
  • 吹就卧 - Means 'blow out the lamp and lie down.'
  • 月色照屋梁，反侧不寐 - Describes the moonlight shining on the roof beams, causing the person to toss and turn unable to sleep.
  • 俄闻厅格扇开 - Means 'soon hear the hall's sliding doors open.'
  • 雨亭起舐窗 - Means 'Rain Pavilion stands up and looks out the window.'
  • 见朝冠老少者三，簪凤衣帔妇女亦如之 - Describes seeing three elderly and young men wearing official hats, and women with phoenix-adorned headdresses and robes.
  • 蹀躞庭院，如有隐忧 - Means 'they walk back and forth in the courtyard as if they have hidden worries.'
  • 其老者以手指西舍 - Means 'the elderly one points towards the west room.'
  • 馀皆西望，作点首状，怅怅良久 - Means 'the others all look westward, nodding their heads, and sigh for a long time.'
  • 互相嗟叹而返 - Means 'they sigh together and return.'
  • 少间，又一妇人白衣缞绖，行至西舍，若欲启扉 - Describes a woman in white mourning clothes walking to the west room, as if she wants to open the door.
  • 雨亭方无所计，忽厅格响，妇乃逡巡退归 - Means 'Rain Pavilion has no idea what to do, but suddenly hears the hall's sliding doors open, and the woman hesitates and returns.'
  • 既而厅格顿合，雨亭心甫定，气稍舒 - Means 'soon after, the hall's sliding doors close, and Rain Pavilion's heart calms down, and his breath eases.'
  • 思出寻仆归 - Means 'thinks of going out to find his servant and return.'
  • 欻西北甬道中，一鬼突如其来，凶恶无伦 - Describes a ghost suddenly appearing in the northwest corridor, extremely fierce and evil.
  • 衣黑缕，咻咻而前，举首对月，则须发交而血模糊也 - Means 'dressed in black rags, he walks forward with a hissing sound, raising his head to the moon, his hair and beard tangled and bloody.'
  • 低首望西舍，似知有人，喜而跃，抵门，洞开 - Means 'lowering his head to look at the west room, he seems to know there is someone, and happily jumps, reaching the door and opening it wide.'
  • 入，左右视，目瞪瞪，见雨亭 - Means 'entering, he looks around, his eyes wide open, seeing Rain Pavilion.'
  • 雨亭不敢视，以手掩面，拳曲榻头 - Means 'Rain Pavilion dares not look, covering his face with his hand, curling up at the head of the bed.'
  • 鬼初作扑攫状、招手状，继乃作嘻笑状、哭泣状，终复大嗥，满屋跃跳，更无状不作 - Describes the ghost initially making grabbing and beckoning gestures, then laughing and crying, and finally roaring loudly, jumping around the whole room, doing nothing else.
  • 雨亭浑身立冰雪，心怔忡出顶际，两太阳凭空乱钟磬声 - Means 'Rain Pavilion feels as if standing in ice and snow, his heart palpitates to the top of his head, and the sun seems to be ringing bells and gongs randomly.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 良久 - Temporal expression meaning 'for a long time' or 'after a long while.'
  • 鸡唱 - Literally 'the rooster crows,' used to indicate the dawn or the start of a new day.
  • 张皇遁去 - '张皇' means 'to panic' or 'to be discomfited,' and '遁去' means 'to flee away.' Together, it describes a panicked retreat.
  • 万籁甫寂 - '万籁' means 'all sounds,' and '甫寂' means 'just quiet.' Together, it describes the moment when all sounds have just fallen silent.
  • 蹶然起 - '蹶然' means 'to startle' or 'to leap up,' and '起' means 'to rise.' Together, it describes a sudden, startled rising.
  • 振衣 - Means 'to shake off one's clothes,' often used to describe the act of getting ready to leave or move quickly.
  • 蹑履 - Means 'to put on shoes,' often used in the context of getting ready to go somewhere.
  • 开戶奔 - '開戶' means 'to open the door,' and '奔' means 'to run.' Together, it describes the act of opening the door and running out.
  • 仆方饮罢 - '仆' refers to a servant, '方' means 'just,' and '饮罢' means 'finished drinking.' Together, it describes the servant just finishing a drink.
  • 自外归 - '自外' means 'from outside,' and '归' means 'to return.' Together, it describes returning from outside.
  • 朦胧视扶其主人 - '朦胧' means 'drowsy' or 'half-asleep,' '视' means 'to see,' and '扶其主人' means 'to support his master.' Together, it describes the servant, drowsy and half-asleep, seeing and supporting his master.
  • 喃喃作醉中语 - '喃喃' means 'mumbling,' and '作醉中语' means 'speaking like someone drunk.' Together, it describes the master mumbling as if he were drunk.
  • 狼狈起 - '狼狈' means 'in a disheveled state,' and '起' means 'to rise.' Together, it describes the master rising in a disheveled state.
  • 气转若游丝 - '气转' means 'the breath turns,' and '若游丝' means 'like a thread.' Together, it describes the master's breath becoming weak and feeble.
  • 侵晓入内 - '侵晓' means 'just before dawn,' and '入内' means 'to enter the house.' Together, it describes entering the house just before dawn.
  • 细述所见 - '细述' means 'to describe in detail,' and '所见' means 'what was seen.' Together, it describes describing in detail what was seen.
  • 询之历历不爽 - '询之' means 'to ask about it,' '历历' means 'clearly,' and '不爽' means 'without error.' Together, it describes asking about it clearly and without error.
  • 甬道鬼是其悍奴受笞自戕者 - '甬道鬼' refers to a ghost in the passageway, '其悍奴' means 'his fierce servant,' '受笞' means 'received a beating,' and '自戕者' means 'who committed suicide.' Together, it describes the ghost as the servant who committed suicide after receiving a beating.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 事甚诡谲，而笔能达之，故佳 - The matter is very strange and elusive, but the writing can express it well, hence it is good. '诡谲' (guǐ jué) means strange or mysterious, and '笔能达之' (bǐ néng dá zhī) means the writing can express it.
  • 自署私居一联云 - Self-inscribed couplet for a private residence. '自署' (zì shǔ) means self-inscribed, and '私居' (sī jū) means private residence.
  • 蠹腹食残经典，马蹄踏尽烟花 - Metaphor for devouring classical texts ('蠹腹食残经典' - 'worm's belly devouring classical texts') and the destruction of beauty or culture ('马蹄踏尽烟花' - 'horse hooves have trampled all the flowers').
  • 不堪教谕，不足训导 - Cannot bear teaching, not worthy of instruction. '不堪教谕' (bù kān jiào yù) means cannot bear teaching, and '不足训导' (bù zú xùn dǎo) means not worthy of instruction.
  • 傲我乾坤醉复顽 - Defiant of the universe, drunk and stubborn. '傲我乾坤' (ào wǒ qián kūn) means defiant of the universe, and '醉复顽' (zuì fù wán) means drunk and stubborn.
  • 惊他岁月去难还 - Startled by the passing years, difficult to return. '惊他岁月' (jīng tā suì yuè) means startled by the passing years, and '去难还' (qù nán huán) means difficult to return.
  • 人生安得元宵死 - How could one in life die on the Lantern Festival. '人生安得' (rén shēng ān dé) means how could one in life, and '元宵死' (yuán xiāo sǐ) means die on the Lantern Festival.
  • 一路灯光到冥关 - A path of lights to the gate of the nether world. '一路灯光' (yī lù dēng guāng) means a path of lights, and '到冥关' (dào míng guān) means to the gate of the nether world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨大本，号椒水，邑庠生 - 杨大本 (Yáng Dàběn) is the name of the person, '号椒水' (hào Jiāoshuǐ) is his pseudonym, and '邑庠生' (yì xiáng shēng) means a student of the local school.
  • 颠于诗，复狂于酒 - Mad about poetry, also crazy about wine. '颠于诗' (diān yú shī) means mad about poetry, and '复狂于酒' (fù kuáng yú jiǔ) means also crazy about wine.
  • 采薇非耻周人粟，颁胙能争孔子豚 - Refers to the story of Bo Yi and Shu Qi who refused to eat grain from the Zhou dynasty ('采薇非耻' - 'cǎi wēi fēi chǐ'), and the story of Confucius and his disciples competing for a piglet ('颁胙能争' - 'bān zuò néng zhēng').
  • 三月可怜忘肉味，萧萧苜蓿掩黉门 - Three months of pity, the sound of alfalfa, blocking the school gate. '三月可怜' (sān yuè kě lián) means three months of pity, '萧萧苜蓿' (xiāo xiāo mù xu) means the sound of alfalfa, and '掩黉门' (yǎn hóng mén) means blocking the school gate.
  • 陶观察署 - Office or residence of Tao Guancha. '陶观察' (Táo Guānchá) is a title, and '署' (shǔ) means office or residence.
  • 一拳打破支机石，两手拆坍乌鹊桥 - Refers to a stone used for writing ('支机石' - 'zhī jī shí') and the magpie bridge in Chinese mythology ('乌鹊桥' - 'wū què qiáo').
  • 四十鳏夫犹未返 - Forty bachelor men still not returned. '四十鳏夫' (sì shí guān fū) means forty bachelor men, and '犹未返' (yóu wèi fǎn) means still not returned.

Proper Nouns

  • 椒水 - His pseudonym.

  • 陶观察 - A title.
  • 支机石 - A stone used for writing.
  • 乌鹊桥 - The magpie bridge in Chinese mythology.

  • 忽见门枨下露妇人足 - '忽见' means 'suddenly see,' and '门枨下' refers to the threshold or base of the door. '露妇人足' means 'the foot of a woman is exposed.'
  • 心忆其来 - '心忆' means 'remember in one's heart,' and '其来' means 'her coming.'
  • 佯睡 - Means 'pretend to sleep.'
  • 女已入室，且倚其床云 - '女已入室' means 'the woman has already entered the room,' and '且倚其床云' means 'and leans on the bed saying.'
  • 路远弓窄，尔先我多时至 - '路远弓窄' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'the road is long and narrow,' suggesting a difficult journey. '尔先我多时至' means 'you arrived much earlier than I did.'
  • 佣不答 - Means 'the servant does not answer.'
  • 尔无怖 - Means 'you have nothing to fear.'
  • 我固非人，然不为尔害，实与尔有夙缘 - '我固非人' means 'I am not a human,' and '然不为尔害' means 'but I will not harm you.' '实与尔有夙缘' means 'I have a predestined connection with you.'
  • 我亦善经理 - Means 'I am also good at managing.'
  • 垂白母，我事之，环堵室，负郭田，我当为尔办 - '垂白母' means 'an elderly mother,' '环堵室' means 'a simple, modest house,' and '负郭田' means 'land near the city wall.'
  • 何必向玉川先生家作裹头奴一世哉 - '玉川先生家' refers to a specific family, and '裹头奴' means 'a servant.' The phrase suggests that the servant should not spend his life as a servant in someone else's household.
  • 此事当告我母，许则遂，不许则已，我不敢擅专 - '此事当告我母' means 'this matter should be reported to my mother,' '许则遂' means 'if she agrees, then it will be done,' and '不许则已' means 'if she does not agree, then it will not be done.' '我不敢擅专' means 'I dare not act on my own.'
  • 请俟异日 - Means 'please wait for another day.'

  • 女怒曰 - Means 'the woman angrily said.'
  • 老奴太无礼！女流在，何亵 - '老奴太无礼' means 'the old servant is too rude,' '女流在' means 'a woman is present,' and '何亵' means 'how disrespectful.'
  • 佣为告免 - Means 'the servant pleads for forgiveness.'
  • 女不得已，订之而贻以一物，嘱勿令他人见，言讫而灭 - '女不得已' means 'the woman reluctantly,' '订之' means 'agreed to,' '贻以一物' means 'gave him an item,' '嘱勿令他人见' means 'instructed him not to let others see it,' and '言讫而灭' means 'after speaking, she vanished.'

  • 后女子每夜必至，求媒合 - Means 'later, the girl would come every night, asking for a matchmaker,' indicating a persistent request.
  • 母颇厌患之，无能治 - Means 'the mother was very annoyed by this and could not find a solution,' indicating a problem that is difficult to resolve.

  • 过仲太史家，言其事而异，继而疑 - Means 'passed by the home of Zhong Taishi and spoke about the matter, finding it strange and then doubtful,' indicating a reaction to the story.
  • 终乃呼其母子而告之曰 - Means 'finally, he called the mother and son and told them,' indicating a final decision to speak.
  • 夫鬼，人为之也 - Means 'ghosts are created by people,' indicating a philosophical or skeptical view.
  • 人能为鬼，鬼即可以为人 - Means 'if people can become ghosts, then ghosts can also become people,' indicating a reciprocal relationship.
  • 使人即与人合，而以鬼道处其人，则人亦与鬼近矣 - Means 'if a person becomes one with another person, but treats them as if they were a ghost, then the person will also become close to being a ghost,' indicating a moral or ethical warning.
  • 苟人而与鬼合而以人道交，其鬼则鬼特即为人用，即人也，何鬼之有 - Means 'if a person becomes one with a ghost and treats them as a person, then the ghost will only be used by the person, and will be a person, so what ghost is there?' indicating a philosophical or skeptical view.

  • 今夜天德合，河魁不房，无再诿 - Means 'tonight, the heavenly virtue is harmonious, and the river chief is not in a position to obstruct, so do not delay,' indicating a favorable time for action.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此条见盛百二补入《济宁志》 - This phrase indicates that the information was added to the *Jining Gazetteer* by someone named Sheng Bai'er. '此条' means 'this entry,' '见' means 'is seen in,' '补入' means 'added to,' and '《济宁志》' refers to the *Jining Gazetteer*.
  • 金酒缸 - A nickname given to a person, likely referring to a magistrate or official named Jin. The term '酒缸' (wine vat) is used metaphorically to describe his drinking habits, suggesting he was heavily involved in alcohol consumption.
  • 奉调入帘 - This phrase means 'appointed to enter the examination hall,' which was a common term for officials who were sent to oversee the imperial examinations.
  • 摄篆务 - '摄' means 'to take charge of temporarily,' and '篆务' refers to administrative duties. Together, it means 'to temporarily handle administrative affairs.'
  • 当其喜则以糊涂了事 - This idiom means 'when pleased, he would settle matters carelessly.' '糊涂' means 'careless' or 'confused,' and '了事' means 'to settle or finish something.'
  • 其怒则捶楚交下 - This phrase means 'when angry, he would beat people mercilessly.' '捶楚' refers to beating, and '交下' means 'to descend upon' or 'to rain down.'
  • 名曰“金酒缸” - This means 'named him 'Jin Wine Vat.' '名曰' means 'to name as' or 'to call.'
  • 登堂以大瓢置案头 - This phrase describes the scene where Jin placed a large gourd (used for drinking) on the desk when he entered the hall. '登堂' means 'to enter the hall,' and '置案头' means 'to place on the desk.'
  • 堂上翁今满载归 - This phrase is a sarcastic remark, meaning 'the old man in the hall is now returning fully loaded.' '堂上翁' refers to the magistrate, and '满载归' suggests he is drunk or overly satisfied.
  • 真可谓名教之罪人也 - This phrase means 'truly he is a great sinner against proper conduct.' '名教' refers to Confucian moral principles, and '罪人' means 'sinner.'
  • 暴卒 - This means 'died suddenly.' '暴' means 'sudden,' and '卒' means 'death.'
  • 尸未殓 - This means 'the body had not yet been buried.' '尸' means 'body,' and '未殓' means 'not yet buried.'
  • 逻守 - This means 'to guard or watch.' '逻' means 'to patrol,' and '守' means 'to guard.'
  • 席地共饮 - This phrase means 'sat on the ground and drank together.' '席地' means 'to sit on the ground,' and '共饮' means 'to drink together.'
  • 背尸坐 - This means 'sitting with their backs to the body.' '背' means 'back,' and '尸' means 'body.'
  • 西向者 - This refers to 'the one facing west.' '西' means 'west,' and '向' means 'facing.'
  • 见尸下 - This means 'saw the body rise.' '见' means 'saw,' and '尸下' means 'the body rose.'
  • 群舁之归 - This means 'carried him back collectively.' '群' means 'collectively,' and '舁之归' means 'carried him back.'
  • 挂锞哀杖 - This refers to 'a ceremonial stick used for beating.' '挂锞' refers to a ceremonial object, and '哀杖' means 'a stick for beating.'
  • 牢不可破 - This means 'firmly fixed and unbreakable.' '牢' means 'firmly,' and '不可破' means 'unbreakable.'
  • 喉间 - This means 'throat.' '喉' means 'throat,' and '间' means 'in.'
  • 呃于嗓 - This means 'stuck in the throat.' '呃' means 'stuck,' and '嗓' means 'throat.'
  • 为之抉出 - This means 'removed it.' '为' means 'for,' '之' means 'it,' and '抉出' means 'to remove.'
  • 而气亦不复续 - This means 'and his breath also did not continue.' '而' means 'and,' '气' means 'breath,' '亦' means 'also,' and '不复续' means 'did not continue.'
  • 探其喉间 - This means 'examined his throat.' '探' means 'examined,' and '喉间' means 'throat.'
  • 有一小粗磁酒杯 - This means 'there was a small, rough ceramic wine cup.' '有' means 'there was,' '一小' means 'small,' '粗' means 'rough,' '磁' means 'ceramic,' and '酒杯' means 'wine cup.'
  • 贪主人之馀沥者 - This means 'someone who coveted the owner's leftover liquor.' '贪' means 'coveted,' '主人' means 'owner,' '之' means 'of,' '馀沥' means 'leftover liquor,' and '者' means 'person.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盛百二 - This refers to Sheng Bai'er, a person who added this entry to the *Jining Gazetteer*. The *Jining Gazetteer* is a historical record of the city of Jining in Shandong Province.
  • 济宁志 - The *Jining Gazetteer* is a local history book that documents the events, people, and places of Jining.
  • 奉调入帘 - This term refers to officials who were appointed to oversee the imperial examinations, which were held in the examination halls (帘).
  • 摄篆务 - This term refers to the temporary handling of administrative duties, often by officials who were not in their permanent positions.
  • 名教 - This term refers to Confucian moral principles, which were highly valued in traditional Chinese society.
  • 暴卒 - This term refers to sudden death, often implying an unexpected or mysterious cause.
  • 尸未殓 - This term refers to the state of a body that has not yet been buried, which was a common practice in traditional Chinese funerary customs.
  • 逻守 - This term refers to the act of guarding or watching, often in the context of protecting a place or person.
  • 席地共饮 - This term refers to the practice of sitting on the ground and drinking together, which was a common social activity in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 背尸坐 - This term refers to the act of sitting with one's back to a body, which could be interpreted as a sign of disrespect or fear.
  • 西向者 - This term refers to the direction of west, which was often associated with the afterlife or the underworld in Chinese culture.
  • 见尸下 - This term refers to the act of seeing a body rise, which could be interpreted as a supernatural event or a sign of possession.
  • 群舁之归 - This term refers to the act of carrying someone back collectively, which could be interpreted as a sign of respect or a response to a crisis.
  • 挂锞哀杖 - This term refers to a ceremonial stick used for beating, which was often used in traditional Chinese rituals.
  • 牢不可破 - This term refers to something that is firmly fixed and unbreakable, which could be interpreted as a sign of strength or a curse.
  • 喉间 - This term refers to the throat, which was often associated with speech, breath, and life in Chinese culture.
  • 呃于嗓 - This term refers to something that is stuck in the throat, which could be interpreted as a sign of choking or a curse.
  • 为之抉出 - This term refers to the act of removing something, which could be interpreted as a sign of relief or a curse being lifted.
  • 而气亦不复续 - This term refers to the act of breathing stopping, which could be interpreted as a sign of death or a curse taking effect.
  • 探其喉间 - This term refers to the act of examining the throat, which could be interpreted as a sign of investigation or a curse being revealed.
  • 有一小粗磁酒杯 - This term refers to a small, rough ceramic wine cup, which could be interpreted as a sign of poverty or a curse.
  • 贪主人之馀沥者 - This term refers to someone who coveted the owner's leftover liquor, which could be interpreted as a sign of greed or a curse.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is a straightforward statement of fact, but the phrase '见盛百二补入' could be interpreted as either 'seen by Sheng Bai'er' or 'added by Sheng Bai'er.'
  • This nickname is a metaphorical description of Jin's drinking habits, but it could be interpreted as either a literal description or a metaphor.
